article.title: Public Works truck driver Lindon Whitehall dies in motorcycle accident
article.text:
Public Works truck driver Lindon Whitehall dies in motorcycle accident
Public Works employee Lindon A. Whitehall died in a motorcycle crash Thursday evening in suburban West Palm Beach.
Whitehall was traveling north above the posted speed limit on Military Trail near Wallis Road when he swerved to avoid a car that was changing lanes in front of him about 7:40 p.m., the Palm Beach County Sheriff’s Office reported.
He lost control of his motorcycle and was struck by a car traveling south, the report said. He was taken to St. Mary’s Medical Center where he was pronounced dead.
No one was cited in the crash. The driver of the southbound car sustained minor injuries, the PBSO said.
Whitehall, 46, had been with the town for about five years, working as a truck driver with the Public Works Department yard trash crew, town officials said.
He was married and had two children.
“His family was the focus of his life,” Public Works Director Paul Brazil said.
“He was a very, very nice guy,” Brazil said. “Nobody is taking this well. They lost a member of their family.”
Counseling has been offered to Whitehall’s co-workers, Brazil said.
article.author: Margie Kacoha

article.title: WAKULLA COUNTY
article.text:
One dead, one critically
injured after Saturday
crash in Wakulla County
WAKULLA CO, FL – A 49-year-old Monticello man was killed and his passenger critically injured Saturday afternoon when his motorcycle drove into a pickup truck traveling ahead of them as it was making a left turn.
The motorcyclist was going east on US 98 at 6:59 p.m. when the pickup started to turn onto Spring Creek Highway.
According to the Florida Highway Patrol, the motorcyclist traveled into the westbound lane to pass the truck within the intersection and struck the side of the pickup. The impact ejected the pair from the motorcycle. Both landed in the westbound lane. The bike came to final rest on the westbound shoulder as the pickup driver came to a controlled stop in the intersection.
The Monticello man was pronounced dead at the scene. His passenger, identified as a 48-year-old Monticello woman, was transported to Tallahassee Memorial Hospital. Neither was wearing a helmet.
The driver of the pickup was a 19-year-old male from Sopchoppy who was traveling with a 15-year-old Crawfordville girl. Both were wearing seatbelts and escaped injury.
FHP was assisted on the scene by the Wakulla County Sheriff’s Office and Wakulla County Fire Rescue.

article.title: Woman, 6 children eject onto road after US 19 crash in Pasco County
article.text:
PASCO COUNTY, Fla. — A woman and six children were transported to the hospital following a crash that left their vehicle flipped upside down.
The Florida Highway Patrol (FHP) says a 33-year-old Hudson woman was driving northbound on U.S. 19 in a Nissan Cube at around 11:30 a.m. Wednesday with the children in the car. A 42-year-old Hudson man was also driving on the highway going southbound in a Toyota Tacoma.
At the intersection of State Road 52, the Toyota driver began to turn left once the light turned green, which is when officials say the Nissan drove in the way of his vehicle, causing the crash.
FHP says the Nissan failed to stop at a red light.
Upon impact, the woman's vehicle overturned, causing her and the six children to eject onto the road. The Toyota stopped on the northbound lanes of U.S. 19.
The crash closed portions of the highway for less than two hours.
The children, ranging from ages five and nine years old, and the woman were sent to a nearby hospital for non-life-threatening injuries.
The 42-year-old man also suffered minor injuries but was not transported.
article.author: Bryce Johnson

article.title: 1 dead, 2 injured in Polk County crash; SR-60 closed
article.text:
LAKE WALES, Fla. (WFLA) — One person died and two others were injured after a vehicle crash in Lake Wales on Friday.
At 12:55 p.m., Deputies with the Polk County Sheriff’s Office and Polk County Fire Rescue were dispatched to State Road 60 and Tiger Lake Road for a traffic cash.
The crash involved a single vehicle that had overturned.
PCSO reported that one adult was killed, and two others were airlifted by helicopters to two separate hospitals.
As of 2:45 p.m., State Road 60 was closed in both directions.
Drivers should keep this in mind and find alternative routes.
article.author: Alaina Papazian

article.title: Fatal, fiery big rig crash snarls traffic on US 1 in lower Florida Keys
article.text:
MIAMI -- Deputies with the Monroe County Sheriff's Office on Tuesday closed a portion of U.S. 1 in the lower Florida Keys after a fatal, fiery predawn traffic accident involving a big rig, authorities said.
The accident occurred shortly after 4:30 a.m. on Sugar Loaf Key near Mile Mark 21 and led to the closure of north- and southbound lanes while crews worked to extinguish a blaze and remove the debris.
As of 8 a.m., it appeared that traffic in the southbound lane was slowly starting to move again,
Officials did not immediately provide details about what led to the crash, but authorities said one person was killed.
Monroe County officials said there would be no trash or recycling service today from mile marker 21 to the 7 Mile Bridge due to this fatal accident.
Tuesday trash and recycling services will take place on Wednesday, authorities said.
There will be no yard waste pickup tomorrow, Wednesday, Dec 6. Wednesday yard waste pick up will resume next Wednesday, Dec 13, county officials said.
Motorists were urged to avoid the area if possible.
article.author: Alfred Charles

article.title: One deceased and others severely injured in Alachua County crash
article.text:
ALACHUA COUNTY, Fla. — Today at approximately 1:05 PM, Florida Highway Patrol (FHP) responded to a fatal two-vehicle traffic crash.
The area of SR 121 & NW 144th Ave was a head-on collision scene.
OPD later determined that the vehicles traveled in opposite directions, resulting in the collision.
One driver was pronounced deceased on scene by Alachua County Fire Rescue. The occupants in the second vehicle sustained serious injuries and were transported to UF Health Shands in Gainesville.
FHP has disclosed that the matter is ongoing and still being investigated.
State Road 121 was closed for several hours to conduct the investigation.

article.title: Motorcyclist killed in early Wednesday morning Osceola County crash
article.text:
Shortly before 6am on Wednesday, a St. Cloud motorcyclist was fatally struck by a car that turned directly into his path on US 192 in Eastern Osceola County.
According to the FHP, the crash happened around 5:55 a.m. on U.S. Highway 192 and Old Melbourne Highway when a sedan driving north on Old Melbourne Highway attempted to turn left onto U.S. Highway 192. The sedan pulled directly into the path of the motorcycle, striking the motorcycle and throwing the rider off the bike, the FHP said.
The FHP said the motorcyclist, a 38-year-old man from Saint Cloud, was pronounced dead at the scene. The driver of the sedan was a 23-year-old woman who was wearing her seat belt and sustained minor injuries.
The crash remains under investigation.

article.title: Parrish man killed in fatal US 301 crash in Manatee County, FHP says
article.text:
A truck hit and killed a 19-year-old Parrish man over the weekend on U.S. 301 in Manatee County, Florida Highway Patrol said in a news release.
The crash happened around 9:38 a.m. Saturday, south of Prosperity Lakes Boulevard, which is less than a mile south of Buckeye Road in Parrish.
Troopers say the 19-year-old pedestrian was in the southbound lane of U.S. 301 while a pickup truck was driving south.
The front right side of the truck hit the Parrish man, the agency said, and he died at the scene.
The truck driver, a 31-year-old Wimauma man, was uninjured, FHP said.
An investigation into the crash is ongoing, troopers said.

article.title: Wrong-way crash on Alligator Alley kills 2, including an off-duty Miccosukee officer
article.text:
A wrong-way crash on Interstate 75, just west of the U.S. 27 exit on Alligator Alley, has killed a Miccosukee police officer and another driver, early Saturday morning.
The crash is still under investigation.
Florida Highway Patrol Lt. Alvaro Feola said Curtis Woolwine, 32, was driving westbound on the eastbound left lane of I-75 south in his black 2016 black Chevy Sonic.
The officer, Steven Greco, 40, was driving to his home in Davie from work in his black 2018 Dodge Charger around 5 a.m. when the crash occurred.
The off-duty officer, who had to be extricated from his car, was airlifted to Broward Health Hospital in Fort Lauderdale where he died. Woolwine died on the scene, Feola said.
FHP is investigating whether Woolwine, who lived in Hollywood, had been drinking before the accident.
Debris from the cars scattered over the lanes and grassy median, closing the roadway for hours.
According to the FHP, Greco was traveling eastbound in the left lane of I-75 South when Woolwine’s Sonic smashed into his Charger’s front end.
This story was originally published February 16, 2019 at 11:01 AM.
article.author: Howard Cohen

article.title: Single vehicle fatal car accident on I-95 in Broward County
article.text:
Single vehicle fatal car accident on I-95 in Broward County
HOLLYWOOD, Fla. (CBS12) — One woman is dead and another has serious injuries after a single vehicle car crash near I-95 near the southbound entrance of State Road 824 (Pembroke Road).
The Florida Highway Patrol (FHP) says at around 3:30 a.m. Saturday a 25-year-old woman is dead after being a passenger in a single vehicle accident, and the 22-year-old woman has sustained serious injuries. Next of kin for both of the women have been notified.
According to FHP, the women's vehicle, a sedan, was traveling southbound on the outside left turn lane of the exit ramp on I-95 to Pembroke Road.
FHP says the vehicle failed to do a left turn to travel eastbound on Pembroke Road and continued travel through the intersection of Pembroke Road and I-95.
The vehicles undercarriage then collided with the curb located on the outside shoulder of Pembroke Road . The vehicle continued to travel south before it collided with a black sign support pole located in the unpaved grass shoulder of Pembroke Road.
FHP says, the vehicle rotated counterclockwise as it was redirected and continued to travel south.
The right side of the vehicle then collided with a concrete sign support pole. The vehicle redirected, again, rotating clockwise.
The vehicle came to a rest south of Pembroke Road and east of the entrance ramp on I-95 on the grass shoulder.
Florida Highway Patrol is investigating the accident.
article.author: Gershon Harrell

article.title: 2 men killed in wrong-way crash that shut down Florida's Turnpike at Commercial Blvd in Broward
article.text:
Two men were killed in a wrong-way crash that completely shut down Florida's Turnpike northbound in Broward County early Monday morning, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
The fatal crash happened at 12:53 a.m. about half a mile south of State Road 814 (Atlantic Boulevard), FHP said.
A driver in an Acura TL sedan was going the wrong way in the northbound lanes when he collided with two people in a Mercedes A220 sedan, authorities said.
The Acura spun out, hit a concrete barrier wall and caught on fire. The driver died at the scene. His name was not immediately released.
The Mercedes also spun out. That driver, a 19-year-old man from Oviedo, was also killed. A 22-year-old passenger from Key West was "incapacitated" due to his injuries and was rushed to North Broward Medical center, FHP said.
Video of the scene showed at least two vehicles smashed into small pieces. As authorities worked the scene, a blue tarp went up near one of the cars.
Meanwhile, drivers were forced off at Exit 62 for Commercial Boulevard/State Road 870. They were advised to take U.S. 441 as an alternate route, as a crash on Interstate 95 was also causing delays.
This is a developing story. Refresh for updates.
article.author: Johanna Torres; Briana Trujillo

article.title: FHP: 1 dead, 2 seriously injured after crash in Jackson County
article.text:
JACKSON COUNTY, Fla. (WTXL) — Troopers are investigating after a fatal crash in Jackson County.
According to Florida Highway Patrol, the accident happened just before 8 p.m. on Sunday at the intersection of US 90 and Russ Street.
FHP says a sedan traveling westbound on US 90 and an SUV was traveling eastbound on US 90 in the inside lane.
The 27-year-old driver of the sedan failed to maintain a single lane and traveled into the eastbound lane in the direct path of the SUV, according to the report.
The cars struck head-on. After the impact, the sedan rotated and came final rest in the eastbound lanes facing an eastbound direction. The SUV came to final rest on the eastbound shoulder facing a southbound direction.
Due to the injuries sustained in the crash, the man driving the sedan was pronounced deceased on scene by Jackson County EMS. The 35-year-old female driving the SUV sustained critical injuries while her passenger, a 5-year-old boy sustained serious injuries.
This crash is still currently under investigation.

Road is closed.

article.title: 1 dead after fiery crash on US 301 in Hillsborough County
article.text:
HILLSBOROUGH COUNTY, Fla. — One person is dead and three others were injured after a fiery crash on U.S. 301, according to the Florida Highway Patrol (FHP).
It happened just past midnight Saturday in the Thonotosassa area, near Jackson Road.
According to FHP, a 22-year-old Brandon woman was driving south on 301 in a Chevy Camaro when she crossed the centerline and collided with a GMC Terrain.
The impact sent both vehicles spinning, coming to rest in the northbound lanes of 301 and the roadway shoulder. That's when state troopers say a third vehicle, a Toyota Camry travelling northbound, collided with the Camaro.
The GMC reportedly caught fire post-collision, and first responders worked to remove the driver, who suffered non-life-threatening injuries.
A passenger in the GMC was unable to be removed from the vehicle and suffered fatal injuries at the scene, officials say. The other two drivers also sustained non-life-threatening injuries.
FHP is asking anyone who witnessed the incident to call *FHP (*347).
article.author: Kaitlyn Snook

article.title: Pedestrian dies in Lauderdale Lakes crash
article.text:
A fatal pedestrian crash shut down northbound and southbound lanes of North State Road 7 in Lauderdale Lakes early Saturday morning.
A little after 4 a.m., a vehicle struck and killed a pedestrian near the intersection of Northwest 19th Street and North State Road 7, according to the Broward Sheriff’s Office. Deputies and Broward Fire Rescue responded, and the victim was pronounced deceased on scene.
The Sheriff’s Office announced that the roadway had reopened around 9:45 a.m.
Traffic homicide detectives investigated the crash, which was not a hit and run, according to Gerdy St. Louis, a spokesperson for the Broward Sheriff’s Office.
“All parties involved in the crash remained on scene,” St. Louis said.
No identities have been released.

article.title: Driver involved in crash that killed UA student, Calera woman was off-duty deputy
article.text:
Driver involved in crash that killed UA student, Calera woman was off-duty deputy
ESCAMBIA COUNTY, Fla. — A tragic incident early Saturday morning claimed the lives of two pedestrians, including a University of Alabama student, on Perdido Key Drive.
The Florida Highway Patrol reported that a 47-year-old Pensacola man was driving east on State Road 292 when he struck two individuals crossing the road just after 3 a.m.
The driver of the vehicle involved has been identified as an off-duty Escambia County Deputy in an unmarked Escambia County Sheriff's Office patrol car, according to the Florida Highway Patrol (FHP).
FHP says the deputy had been returning home from an off-duty job at the time of the crash. The deputy has been placed on administrative leave, per policy.
"They did not activate the crosswalk lights. They went across dark," Commissioner Steve Stroberger said. "And I think there was an EMS vehicle that was actually en route to another call and they actually witnessed it. And they noticed that it hadn't been activated." He noted the dark crosswalks are hard to see. Stroberger explained some lights go out before you finish crossing. He is discussing safety enhancements with FDOT.
Sources with The University of Alabama confirmed that Jackson Cunningham, a University of Alabama student and member of the Theta Chi fraternity, is one of the people killed. The other victim is 20-year-old Nicole Moore from Calera according to reports by WEAR-TV. Both were pronounced dead at the scene.
Theta Chi Alabama shared on social media that Cunningham, a native of Hoover, Alabama, was a member of the pledge class of 2022. The post described him as "a man of unwavering character, faith, and kindness," adding that "his impact on the lives of others will last forever."
A gofundme has been set up to help the family with funeral expenses.
Paul Mitchell the School in Birmingham posted a tribute to Moore:
Our hearts are heavy as we remember Nicole Moore, one of our bright and beautiful future professionals.
Nicole brought laughter, light, and warmth to every room she walked into. Her sense of humor, talent, and kindness left a lasting impression on everyone students and staff alike.
She will be deeply missed, forever loved, and always remembered.
Rest in peace, Nicole.
article.author: Gracie Johnson; Cynthia Gould

54-year-old man killed in head-on crash in Seminole County, troopers say
Read full article: 54-year-old man killed in head-on crash in Seminole County, troopers saySEMINOLE COUNTY, Fla. – A passenger was killed when two cars were involved in a head-on crash in Seminole County Tuesday afternoon, according to the Florida Highway Patrol. Troopers said a 59-year-old Tampa man was driving a 2012 Ford Focus westbound on State Road 436 at about 4:50 p.m. when for an unknown reason he crossed a median and entered the eastbound lanes. [TRENDING: ‘Tragedy and nightmare:’ 10 killed in Colorado | Fla. woman who coughed on cancer patients could face jail time | AstraZeneca may have used outdated info in vaccine trial]AdOnce on the opposite side of the road, the Focus crashed head on into a 2016 Kia Forte driven by a 19-year-old Apopka woman, records show. Both drivers suffered minor injuries but the 54-year-old Kissimmee man who was a passenger in the Focus died at an area hospital. The crash remains under investigation.
Woman standing in middle of SR-436 in Apopka struck, killed by pickup truck
Read full article: Woman standing in middle of SR-436 in Apopka struck, killed by pickup truckA 21-year-old woman standing in the middle of State Road 436 in Seminole County was struck and killed by a pickup truck Tuesday morning, the Florida Highway Patrol said. 436 at Executive Park Court in Apopka. AdThe driver of the pickup, a 20-year-old Casselberry man, was unable to avoid the woman, according to an FHP report. [WATCH: Highway Patrol urging caution on Central Florida roads after uptick in deadly crashes]The woman was pronounced dead at the scene, according to troopers. The driver, who was wearing a seat belt, was not injured, the FHP said.
Dump truck becomes suspended in air after hitting an overpass, officials say
Read full article: Dump truck becomes suspended in air after hitting an overpass, officials saySEMINOLE COUNTY, Fla. – First responders were called to a crash Saturday after a dump truck hit an overpass. According to officials, the crash caused the front of the truck to be “suspended in the air.”The crash happened on Interstate 4 and State Road 436 near mile marker 92. Driver successfully extricated; DOT engineers en route. “The driver was successfully extricated by crews and was treated for minor injuries at the scene,” first responders said. No lanes were closed to traffic as crews worked to remove the truck.
Here’s how to navigate the latest I-4 traffic shift at Maitland Boulevard
Read full article: Here’s how to navigate the latest I-4 traffic shift at Maitland BoulevardMany drivers missed the latest shift at Maitland Boulevard causing backups along I-4 between Lee Road and State Road 436 as motorists attempted to make a U-Turn at SR 436, according to FDOT. Beginning Wednesday morning, drivers exiting eastbound I-4 to westbound Maitland Boulevard (exit 90B) will begin that shift approximately a quarter of a mile sooner, coupled with the exit point to eastbound Maitland Boulevard (exit 90A). The new combined eastbound exit ramp splits to provide access to both directions of Maitland Boulevard. Eastbound I-4 exit is now in its final configuration and access to eastbound and westbound Maitland Boulevard will remain combined on the same ramp. However, be on the lookout for additional shifts of I-4 ramps at the Maitland interchange, according to FDOT.

article.title: Couple arrested in deadly Seminole County hit-and-run crash
article.text:
Couple arrested in deadly Seminole County hit-and-run crash
BREAKING UPDATES. A HUSBAND AND WIFE ARRESTED FOR A DEADLY HIT AND RUN ON 1792, IN SEMINOLE COUNTY, MADE THEIR FIRST APPEARANCE IN COURT TODAY. FLORIDA HIGHWAY PATROL SAYS THE HUSBAND HIT A BICYCLIST AT A BIKE LANE ON SATURDAY, AND NOW THEY’RE BOTH FACING CHARGES. WESH 2’S MICHELLE MEREDITH IS LIVE AT THE SEMINOLE COUNTY JAIL THIS AFTERNOON. MICHELLE. WELL, TROOPERS SAY THAT THE WIFE IS NOT ACCUSED OF THE HIT AND RUN, THAT SHE WAS NOT INSIDE THE CAR. AT THE SAME TIME, HOWEVER, SHE IS ACCUSED OF DRIVING HER CAR TO THE SCENE, TALKING TO HER HUSBAND, AND THEN BASICALLY ESCORTING THEM BACK TO THEIR HOME. WITH HER LEADING THE WAY IN HER CAR, AND HE IS FOLLOWING BEHIND AND IS DAMAGED. BMW. HE IS HERE ON A KALYN RASH, LEAVING THE SCENE, FAILING TO REMAIN AT A CRASH INVOLVING DEATH, AND AFTER 37 YEAR OLD CHRISTOPHER CONNETT AND HIS WIFE, 39 YEAR OLD BELKIS RODRIGUEZ, TOOK TURNS FACING A JUDGE THURSDAY. BUT NEITHER APPARENTLY WANTED TO FACE THE CAMERA. MR. RODRIGUEZ IS HERE ON A ACCESSORY AFTER THE FACT ON A FIRST DEGREE MISDEMEANOR, ACCORDING TO FLORIDA HIGHWAY PATROL. IT STARTED SATURDAY NIGHT WHEN WITNESSES SAY THE HUSBAND DRIVING A BMW SWERVED INTO THE BIKE LANE ON 1792, IN THE FERN PARK AREA, SENDING THE 25 YEAR OLD CYCLIST FLYING IN THE AIR AND TRAPPING THE MAN’S BIKE UNDER THE BMW. INVESTIGATORS SAY NOT ONLY DID CHRISTOPHER FAIL TO STOP, HE ALLEGEDLY DRAGGED THE BIKE FOR ANOTHER MILE BEFORE PULLING INTO A PARKING LOT. THE ARREST REPORT SAYS AFTER TROOPERS MADE CONTACT WITH THE COUPLE TO TALK TO THEM, THEY WERE SEEN BY NEIGHBORS PACKING THEIR BAGS IN THE BACK OF THE WIFE’S HYUNDAI, THEN LEAVING. EVEN THOUGH THE STATE ARGUED THE COUPLE COULD BE A FLIGHT RISK. BOTH WERE GRANTED BONDS. THEIR ATTORNEY SAYS THE COUPLE IS NOT GOING ANYWHERE AND ARE READY TO DEFEND THEMSELVES. MY CLIENTS ARE NOT A FLIGHT RISK. THEY WANT TO HAVE THEIR DAY IN COURT. THEY’VE RETAINED PRIVATE COUNSEL AND THEY’RE READY TO DEFEND THEMSELVES. WHY DID THEY THINK TO CALL AN ATTORNEY BUT NOT 911? I HAVE NO FURTHER COMMENTS. NOW, ACCORDING TO INVESTIGATORS, THE COUPLE, THEY WERE MAKING PLANS TO FILE AN INSURANCE CLAIM BUT NEVER CALLED LAW ENFORCEMENT. LIVE
Couple arrested in deadly Seminole County hit-and-run crash
Updated: 5:26 PM EDT Aug 21, 2025
Editorial Standards
Two people have been arrested following a fatal hit-and-run in Seminole County on Saturday, according to the Florida Highway Patrol. FHP shared on Wednesday that 37-year-old Christopher Canet was arrested for leaving the scene of a crash involving death. His wife, 39-year-old Belkis Rodriguez, was also arrested for leaving the scene of a crash involving death and accessory after the fact. Both were booked into the Seminole County Jail. FHP said the crash happened on Saturday at 9:46 p.m. when a vehicle traveling northbound on U.S. 17-92 near Spartan Drive hit a bicyclist who was traveling in the marked bicycle lane between the outside travel lane and the right turn lane. A witness told FHP that the vehicle entered the outside northbound travel lane of 17-92 from State Road 414. The vehicle then fled north on 17-92 after the crash.Troopers said the bicyclist, a 25-year-old Winter Springs man, was pronounced dead at the crash scene.FHP investigators located the vehicle involved, a 2016 BMW X6, and it has been taken into evidence. Troopers are still gathering information to determine who was driving the vehicle at the time of the crash.
SEMINOLE COUNTY, Fla. — Two people have been arrested following a fatal hit-and-run in Seminole County on Saturday, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
FHP shared on Wednesday that 37-year-old Christopher Canet was arrested for leaving the scene of a crash involving death. His wife, 39-year-old Belkis Rodriguez, was also arrested for leaving the scene of a crash involving death and accessory after the fact.
Both were booked into the Seminole County Jail.
FHP said the crash happened on Saturday at 9:46 p.m. when a vehicle traveling northbound on U.S. 17-92 near Spartan Drive hit a bicyclist who was traveling in the marked bicycle lane between the outside travel lane and the right turn lane.
A witness told FHP that the vehicle entered the outside northbound travel lane of 17-92 from State Road 414. The vehicle then fled north on 17-92 after the crash.
Troopers said the bicyclist, a 25-year-old Winter Springs man, was pronounced dead at the crash scene.
FHP investigators located the vehicle involved, a 2016 BMW X6, and it has been taken into evidence.
Troopers are still gathering information to determine who was driving the vehicle at the time of the crash.

article.title: 2 men killed in head-on crash in Osceola County, FHP says
article.text:
OSCEOLA COUNTY, Fla. - Two men were killed late Wednesday in a head-on crash in Osceola County.
The crash happened near the intersection of State Road 60 and Mae Bass Road in near Yeehaw Junction, according to Florida Highway Patrol.
A 54-year-old man from Tampa was traveling eastbound on State Road 60 in a Honda CR-V when he entered the westbound lane as he attempted to pass traffic, FHP said.
The Honda CR-V entered into the direct path of a Dodge Ram that was traveling westbound in the westbound lane of State Road 60, troopers said.
The two vehicles collided head-on, according to FHP. The driver of the Honda CR-V was pronounced dead at the scene. The driver of the Dodge Ram, a 52-year-old man from Greenacres, Florida, also died at the scene.
The crash is under investigation.
The Source: This story was written with information from Florida Highway Patrol.

article.title: 1 dead, 1 in critical condition after triple crash on I-95 in Brevard
article.text:
1 dead, 1 in critical condition after triple crash on I-95 in Brevard
Troopers say a good Samaritan stopped to assist that driver and was also struck by a vehicle
Troopers say a good Samaritan stopped to assist that driver and was also struck by a vehicle
The Florida Highway Patrol is investigating after one person died and another is in critical condition after three crashes on Interstate 95 in Titusville.
FHP said the series of crashes occurred shortly after midnight Saturday morning on I-95 northbound south of State Road 46. The first crash occurred when the driver of a Chevrolet Impala changed lanes into the outside lane, directly into the path of a Volkswagen ID.4. The Chevy's driver exited his vehicle after the crash and was subsequently struck by two vehicles that tried to avoid him and his disabled car.
That driver, a 33-year-old Port Saint Lucie man, died at the scene.
Troopers say a good Samaritan stopped to assist that driver and was also struck by a vehicle.
She was airlifted to HCA Lake Monroe Hospital in critical condition.
FHP said the crash remains under investigation.
article.author: Shane Winsten

article.title: 41-year-old man dead following crash in Indian River County
article.text:
41-year-old man dead following crash in Indian River County
GIFFORD, Fla. (CBS12) — A 41-year-old man is dead following a two-vehicle accident at an intersection.
According to the Florida Highway Patrol, the 41-year-old male was traveling northbound in a sport utility vehicle on 58th Avenue, approaching 49th Street.
The driver of the sedan failed to stop at the posted stop sign and entered the intersection of 58th Avenue. The front of her vehicle struck the left side of the male's vehicle.
The vehicle then overturned as the driver was ejected from the vehicle.
The man died on scene.
The case is still pending investigation.
article.author: DJ Siddiqi

article.title: Fort Walton Beach man killed in US 98 crash; police seeking witnesses - Mid Bay News
article.text:
🚲 Jason Turpen, 45, was struck while riding his bicycle west on the sidewalk along US 98
🚑 He was transported to the hospital, where he later died from his injuries
👮 Police are investigating and asking witnesses to contact Officer Stott at 850-833-9546
FORT WALTON BEACH — A Fort Walton Beach man was killed Saturday afternoon after being struck by a pickup truck while riding his bicycle along a sidewalk on U.S. Highway 98, police said.
The incident occurred around 4:31 p.m. on August 2, in front of 236 Miracle Strip Parkway SW, near the Misty Breeze Condominiums.
According to the Fort Walton Beach Police Department, 45-year-old Jason Turpen was riding his bicycle westbound on the south side sidewalk of U.S. 98 when he was hit by a black Chevrolet pickup truck driven by Paul Taylor.
Turpen was transported to HCA Florida Fort Walton-Destin Hospital, where he was later pronounced dead, officials confirmed.
Police have not released additional details about how the crash occurred, but say the investigation remains active.
Anyone who witnessed the incident or has information is urged to contact Officer Stott at 850-833-9546.
Register or login with Mid Bay News and never get another pop up on our site!

article.title: Lutz man arrested for DUI manslaughter following fatal US 41 crash
article.text:
LUTZ, Fla. - One man is dead, and another is behind bars after a crash in Pasco County on Thursday evening.
According to the Florida Highway Patrol, William Edward Ricks, 72, was driving a Ford Escape southbound on US 41 around 6:30 p.m. as a 26-year-old Largo man was riding a Suzuki motorcycle northbound on US 41.
Troopers say Ricks entered the path of the motorcycle when he turned left onto Lake Floyd Drive and the vehicles collided.
The motorcyclist was taken to an area hospital where he later died.
Ricks, according to troopers, was later determined to be impaired and was arrested for DUI manslaughter.
The Source: This story was written with information provided by the Florida Highway Patrol.

article.title: Santa Rosa Beach man dies after traffic accident in Walton County
article.text:
WALTONC COUNTY, Fla. (WMBB) – A Santa Rosa Beach man has died after an accident on State Road 83 at Rock Hill Road in Walton County on Monday around 6:30 a.m.
According to the Florida Highway Patrol, the 31-year-old victim was driving his truck north on SR 83 (US-331) when he failed to stop and collided with the rear of the tractor/trailer in front of him.
The driver of the truck died from injuries he sustained during the crash. The driver of the other vehicle was unharmed.
Officials said both lanes of SR 83 were blocked due to the vehicles involved and the investigation.
article.author: Jakson Hurst

The Florida Highway Patrol said the fatal crash was reported at 7:40 p.m. on I-75 south at mile marker 358.5, north of State Road 326. Troopers said the man was walking in the left lane of southbound I-75 when he was struck by a sport utility vehicle. Miguel Espinoza Navarro, 41, was allegedly waving his gun and shooting at vehicles from a black Ford truck before rear-ending a vehicle, deputies said. Navarro continued driving and then had a standoff with deputies after wrecking his vehicle a second time near mile marker 317, deputies said. After fleeing in his car again, Navarro wrecked his car for a third time and fled on foot, deputies said. No people or deputies were injured during the ordeal, deputies said.

The two vehicles were traveling north on I-75 when a FHP car slowed ahead of them, troopers said. The driver of the first vehicle swerved to avoid a collision with the FHP car and lost control, causing the car to rotate and collide with the left side of the second vehicle, according to troopers. The first vehicle overturned multiple times before coming to a stop, while the second vehicle came to a more controlled stop on the shoulder of the freeway, troopers said. One woman was taken to a hospital with serious injuries, while a boy was taken with minor injuries.

A passenger was killed in a Sunday afternoon crash at State Road 50 and U.S. 301 in Hernando County, Florida Highway Patrol said.
HERNANDO COUNTY, FL — A woman was killed in a Sunday afternoon crash involving a tractor-trailer truck on State Road 50, according to a Florida Highway Patrol news release.
A 72-year-old Nobleton man was driving his car east on SR-50 when he turned left into the path of the truck, driven by a 66-year-old Iowa man, at the intersection of U.S. 301 just before 2 p.m.
The car’s driver was seriously injured in the crash and brought to a local hospital.

article.title: Driver arrested for Highlands County crash that killed 13-year-old boy, injured 2 others
article.text:
LAKE PLACID, Fla. - An 18-year-old involved in a deadly crash while speeding from the Florida Highway Patrol in Highlands County has been arrested.
The backstory:
On January 21, 2025, the Florida Highway Patrol tried to stop a 2021 Kia Forte for speeding on US 27 near Lake Henry Drive in Highlands County.
Troopers say instead of stopping, the Kia sped away.
While speeding from FHP, troopers say the Kia collided with the back of a 2012 Ford E350.
CLICK HERE:>>> Follow FOX 13 on YouTube
Troopers say a 13-year-old boy in the Kia was taken to an area hospital with critical injuries and later died.
Deanthony Torres booking photo courtesy of the St. Lucie County Sheriff's Office.
Two other passengers in the Kia, a 16-year-old boy and a 16-year-old girl, were taken to an area hospital with injuries.
The driver of the Kia, who has since been identified as Deanthony Torres, 18, of Okeechobee, was taken to an area hospital. He was released on Wednesday and booked into the St. Lucie County Jail.
The driver of the Ford E350 was not injured in the crash.
What's next:
Torres is awaiting extradition back to Highlands County.
Torres has been charged with aggravated manslaughter of a child, aggravated fleeing to elude with serious injury or death, driving with a suspended license (serious injury or death) and two counts of attempted murder.
The Source: This story was written with information provided by the Florida Highway Patrol.

article.title: Pickup truck driver dies in Baker County crash on SR 121
article.text:
Pickup truck driver dies in Baker County crash on SR 121
Florida Highway Patrol (WJXT, Copyright 2025 News4JAX - All Rights Reserved)
BAKER COUNTY, Fla. – A 61-year-old man died Sunday morning in Baker County after he veered off the road and overturned on State Road 121.
The accident report said the man was driving a pickup truck south on State Road 121 near Jerry Johns Road as he drove around a curve around 11 a.m.
For some reason, the report said, the man veered off the roadway and drove into a ditch, where he hit a concrete culvert, causing the vehicle to go airborne.
The pickup traveled into the woods and flipped over.
The driver, who the report said was wearing a seatbelt, died at the scene.

article.title: Fatal crashes mount in Lee County
article.text:
Fatal crashes mount in Lee County
Several hit-and-run crashes reported
Two crashes in Lee County on Monday claimed two lives, one a pedestrian struck by a hit-and-run driver and the other involving a Fort Myers city garbage truck.
Those fatalities pushed the county's death toll to 24, one shy of the total for this time last year. Adding in Collier and Hendry counties, nine people were killed and nearly a dozen hurt in crashes on local roadways since Thursday.
On Monday a pedestrian was killed walking along Pine Island Road when he was struck by a hit-and-run driver. A second car could not avoid also hitting the man.
The crash shut down the westbound lanes on Pine Island between Woodward Avenue to U.S. 41 for nearly five hours as the Florida Highway Patrol conducted an investigation.
Police were still trying to contact next of kin for the victim. The FHP said that a possible description of the first vehicle is a 1988 to 1992 Chevrolet full-size pickup or SUV (color unknown) with damage to the right front amber marker lens.
Later on Monday a Fort Myers city garbage truck collided with a van along State Road 82 east of I-75, killing the driver of the van.
State Road 82 from Ortiz Avenue to the freeway and the northbound off-ramp to 82 from I-75 were shut down for several hours while police investigated. Fort Myers police at the scene had no information on the name of the driver or what happened.
A spokesman for the police said they would issue a release later on Monday.
A crash on Sunday critically injured a Fort Myers man, Sundiata Ade Hill, 44.
The FHP reported that Hill was driving a 2007 Toyota Avalon north on Metro Parkway when he lost control on a curve near Landing View Road.
Hill was ejected from the car after it overturned and hit a power line guidewire.
Brittany Thompson, 23, of Cape Coral died after a head-on crash around 11:30 p.m. Saturday on McGregor Boulevard at Poinciana Avenue. Seven people were transported to Lee Memorial Hospital with a variety of injuries. Thompson died at Lee Memorial.
The investigation is still active.
On Friday, two Lee County men died in a crash on State Road 80 in LaBelle.
Nicholas Mankiewicz, 25 of Fort Myers, and 55-year-old Cebert Williams of Lehigh Acres died about 5:40 a.m., according to a Florida Highway Patrol report.
Mankiewicz was driving a 2005 Toyota Tundra west on SR 80 west of Lexington Parkway when he tried to pass "slow traffic," the report said. He crashed head on into Williams' truck when both men swerved to the south shoulder to avoid each other.
The men died at the scene. Neither of them were wearing seatbelts, the report said.
A hit-and-run crash on Friday critically injured Marcario Gaspar, 20, of Fort Myers, as he walked along Palm Beach Boulevard.
An FHP report said that Gaspar walked into the path of a car driving east on Palm Beach near Bellair Road.
The car, which fled the scene, was described as a silver or gray Toyota Camry or Corolla.
The FHP report indicated alcohol was a factor in the crash.
A head-on crash in Collier County killed three people Thursday afternoon.
According to the Florida Highway Patrol, Ayesha Khaleedah Idlette, 31, was driving a 2005 Pontiac Sunfire north on State Road 29 south of Oil Well Road, before traveling into the southbound lanes where she collided with a second vehicle.
Alejandro Rodriguez, 41, of Naples, the driver of the second vehicle, a cargo van, was taken to Naples Community Hospital.
The passenger in the cargo van, 21-year-old Irvin Michael Arellano Rodriguez, also of Naples, was taken to Physicians Regional Hospital where he died.
Information from any of these crashes can be relayed to the Florida Highway Patrol at 239-938-1800
Connect with this reporter: MichaelBraunNP (Facebook) @MichaelBraunNP (Twitter)
article.author: Michael Braun; The News-Press

article.title: Fort Walton Beach man killed in US 98 crash; police seeking witnesses - Mid Bay News
article.text:
🚲 Jason Turpen, 45, was struck while riding his bicycle west on the sidewalk along US 98
🚑 He was transported to the hospital, where he later died from his injuries
👮 Police are investigating and asking witnesses to contact Officer Stott at 850-833-9546
FORT WALTON BEACH — A Fort Walton Beach man was killed Saturday afternoon after being struck by a pickup truck while riding his bicycle along a sidewalk on U.S. Highway 98, police said.
The incident occurred around 4:31 p.m. on August 2, in front of 236 Miracle Strip Parkway SW, near the Misty Breeze Condominiums.
According to the Fort Walton Beach Police Department, 45-year-old Jason Turpen was riding his bicycle westbound on the south side sidewalk of U.S. 98 when he was hit by a black Chevrolet pickup truck driven by Paul Taylor.
Turpen was transported to HCA Florida Fort Walton-Destin Hospital, where he was later pronounced dead, officials confirmed.
Police have not released additional details about how the crash occurred, but say the investigation remains active.
Anyone who witnessed the incident or has information is urged to contact Officer Stott at 850-833-9546.

article.title: Pinellas County Car Crash Kills 52-Year-Old Pedestrian
article.text:
Pinellas County Car Crash Kills 52-Year-Old Pedestrian
There have been 7,042 auto accidents resulting in 66 fatalities within Pinellas County alone since the beginning of this year, according to the latest set of statistics from the Florida Department of Highway Safety and Motor Vehicles. One such incident occurred over the weekend as investigative authorities with the Pinellas County Sheriff’s Office are continuing to work in conjunction with additional local law enforcement agencies in order to determine the chain of events that led to the death of a 52-year-old male pedestrian along Gulf Boulevard in Treasure Island, Florida.
According to statements from the responding officers with Pinellas County Sheriff’s Office, it was shortly before 10 p.m. this past Sunday evening that dispatchers were first notified of a car vs. pedestrian accident right in front of the Penthouse Beach Club located at 10182 Gulf Boulevard. Authorities have indicated that 53-year-old Christopher Grazer was driving a 2006 Dodge Caravan in the southern direction of Gulf Boulevard when 52-year-old Daniel Wells attempted to walk across the same busy street from the east to the west. Shortly after Mr. Wells stepped into the roadway – not in a designated crosswalk area – near the popular beach resort, he was struck by the front passenger side of the Dodge Caravan.
Pinellas County emergency response teams were immediately dispatched to the scene of the deadly accident where they were able to stabilize the pedestrian victim long enough to be transported by ambulance to a local hospital, however, due to the severity of his life-threatening injuries, the 52-year-old sadly passed away shortly after arriving at the trauma center.
The 53-year-old driver of the Dodge Caravan remained at the crash site after the fatal collision occurred and was said to be in full cooperation with the Pinellas County Sheriff’s Office. Although a specific cause has yet to be determined, neither speed nor alcohol are believed to have been a factor in this truly tragic accident.

article.title: Semitruck carrying beer overturns, blocks all southbound lanes on I-95 in St. Johns County
article.text:
Semitruck carrying beer overturns, blocks all southbound lanes on I-95 in St. Johns County Traffic stalled for hours
Semitruck overturns, blocks all southbound lanes in St. Johns County (St. Johns County Sheriff's Office)
ST. JOHNS COUNTY, Fla. – All southbound lanes on Interstate 95 near mile marker 328 from State Road 9B in St. Johns County are closed due to a multi-vehicle crash.
As of 9:30 p.m., lanes remained closed.
A semitruck carrying a truckload of Busch Beer overturned blocking all lanes, the St. Johns Fire Rescue said. Multiple vehicles were involved in the crash on Friday around 6:15 p.m.
Traffic was backed up for miles as emergency crews worked to clean up the scene.
Semitruck carrying Busch Beer overturned on Interstate 95 in St. Johns County (WJXT) According to fire rescue, one person was taken to the hospital in serious but stable condition.
Road officials expect the highway to be shut down for several hours.
Traffic is being diverted to State Road 9B.

article.title: Person killed in chaotic SW Miami-Dade crash identified; suspected driver arrested
article.text:
WESTCHESTER — New details have been released regarding a
that left one person dead and two other people in critical condition on Friday morning.Miami-Dade Police announced in an updated press release Saturday afternoon that they have identified the deceased victim as 34-year-old Daniel Garcia and the suspect driver — 28-year-old Dymariz Picon — was arrested and facing vehicular homicide charges.
According to the arrest affidavit, Picon was driving the white Nissan pickup truck traveling westbound on SW 21st Terrace. Inside the truck with her was Garcia and another occupant known only as Pac. Meanwhile, a GMC van, which was driven by 30-year-old Alexis Gonzalez Martinez and his 26-year-old assistant Jose G. Perez Estevez, was traveling south on SW 92nd Avenue and crossing 21st Terrace.
At this moment, Picon failed to stop at the stop sign ahead "in a willful and wanton disregard for the safety of persons or property," and violently struck the left-front corner of the van. Upon the collision, Picon's truck was redirected west and struck five parked cars in front of nearby homes. After that, Picon and Pac failed to aid everyone involved in the crash and fled the scene, leaving Garcia's lifeless body inside the truck, the affidavit stated.
Further investigation revealed that a Miami-Dade School Board officer was alerted to a possible threat in progress at the On-the-Go Market at 8200 SW 24th Street, where Picon allegedly refused to stop for the school board officer who was attempting a traffic stop with emergency equipment before losing sight of Picon. Moments later, the officer discovered the crash and learned that the vehicle was pursuing earlier was the one involved, the arrest affidavit stated.
Miami-Dade Fire Rescue responded to the scene shortly after it was reported and declared Garcia dead at 9 a.m., and transported both Gonzalez Martinez and Perez Estevez to HCA Kendall Hospital Trauma Center in critical condition.
MDPD was able to identify and track Picon and brought her into their headquarters' homicide bureau, where she confessed to the crash after being given her Miranda rights. According to the arrest affidavit, a records check revealed that not only did Picon not have a Florida driver's license but her state ID card was also issued to a habitual traffic offender in January 2021 for five years.
She was then arrested and charged with driving without a license that caused a deadly crash, fleeing the scene of a deadly crash and property damage.
article.author: Hunter Geisel

article.title: Parrish man killed in fatal US 301 crash in Manatee County, FHP says
article.text:
A truck hit and killed a 19-year-old Parrish man over the weekend on U.S. 301 in Manatee County, Florida Highway Patrol said in a news release.
The crash happened around 9:38 a.m. Saturday, south of Prosperity Lakes Boulevard, which is less than a mile south of Buckeye Road in Parrish.
Troopers say the 19-year-old pedestrian was in the southbound lane of U.S. 301 while a pickup truck was driving south.
The front right side of the truck hit the Parrish man, the agency said, and he died at the scene.
The truck driver, a 31-year-old Wimauma man, was uninjured, FHP said.
An investigation into the crash is ongoing, troopers said.

Man walking on sidewalk fatally struck by vehicle in Ocala
Read full article: Man walking on sidewalk fatally struck by vehicle in OcalaOCALA, Fla. A 22-year-old man walking on the sidewalk was killed when a vehicle veered off the roadway and struck him Tuesday night, according to the Ocala Police Department. Police said William Ryan Abbott was on the sidewalk near State Road 200 and SW 35th Terrace when a vehicle that was on Interstate 75 left the roadway toward the right, traveled onto the sidewalk and grass and struck him. Abbott was taken to an area hospital as a trauma alert, where he died as a result of his injuries. They also said that while the incident is still under investigation, its not suspected that drugs or alcohol played a role in the crash. Police said his death marks the sixth pedestrian fatality in Ocala this year.

Read full article: 3 dead after fiery crash on State Road 200 in Marion CountyMARION COUNTY, Fla. – Two women and a 16-year-old girl were killed in a head-on, fiery crash on State Road 200 in Marion County Friday morning, according to the Florida Highway Patrol. Troopers said a 60-year-old Crystal River man was driving a pickup truck towing a 12-foot trailer eastbound on State Road 200 near County Road 484 around 6:20 a.m. when he failed to maintain his lane and entered into the westbound lane. After the initial crash, troopers said the truck hit the left side of a second sedan that was also in the westbound lane. The 39-year-old woman driving the first sedan and a 16-year-old girl who was in the back seat died at the scene of the crash. The 73-year-old woman driving the second sedan also died at the scene of the crash.

article.title: 78-year-old Ellenton man killed in fatal US 301 crash in Manatee County, troopers say
article.text:
A 78-year-old Ellenton man died in a two-vehicle crash Monday morning at a U.S. 301 intersection, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
Troopers said the man was driving a pickup truck southbound on U.S 301 and attempted to turn left onto Colony Drive South in Ellenton around 6:36 a.m. when he entered the path of a northbound Hyundai Sonata.
The front left of the Sonata crashed into the front right of the truck, causing the pickup to rest on the paved shoulder of U.S. 301, while the sedan ended up on a grassy shoulder, according to a news release.
FHP says paramedics pronounced the pickup driver, who was not wearing a seatbelt, dead at the scene.
The driver of the Hyundai, a 23-year-old Ellenton man, suffered serious but non-life-threatening injuries and was taken to HCA Florida Blake Hospital, according to the release.
The crash remains under investigation, FHP says.
article.author: Michael Moore Jr

article.title: Bradford County crash claims 1, injures 2
article.text:
A Jacksonville woman died when she drove in front of a tractor-trailer at the intersection of County Road 225 and State Road 16 in Bradford County on Friday morning.
According to a Florida Highway Patrol (FHP) report, a 38-year-old female from Jacksonville was driving a 2016 Hyundai sedan north on County Road 225 when she stopped at a posted stop sign at the intersection of State Road 16 at 11:36 a.m. She then proceeded to cross SR 16 when she was hit in the passenger side by a 58-year-old male from Starke driving a 2017 Kenworth tractor-trailer traveling westbound on SR 16.
A 2019 Freightliner with a trailer was in the gas station parking lot in the northwest corner of the intersection of CR 225 and SR 16 and the occupant of the parked Freightliner was unloading the trailer at the time of the collision. After the Kenworth with the Hyundai, it entered the gas station parking lot where it struck the front left of the Freightliner. The Hyundai came to a final rest on SR 16.
Emergency responders transported the Hyundai driver to a hospital in Starke where she was pronounced dead.
The Kenworth driver and an 8-year-old female Hyundai passenger received minor injuries.

article.title: Names of 3 Pedestrians, Driver Killed in Crash Outside Marlins Park Released
article.text:
Police have released the names of the victims of a deadly pedestrian crash outside Marlins Park.
Esther Terrero de Diaz, 53, Franklin Diaz, 14, and Adriana Diaz, 13 were killed after they were struck by an out-of-control red mini-van on NW 7th Street, Miami Police said Monday.
They and Raul Ortega Arias, 67, of Miami – the driver of the 2006 Dodge Caravan who lost control and collided into a building on NW 7th Street and NW 17th Avenue – died shortly after they arrived at Jackson Memorial Hospital’s Ryder Trauma Center Saturday night, police said.
The fourth pedestrian struck, Magdeline Diaz, 10, is in critical condition at Ryder Trauma Center, police said.
Grandmother Esther Diaz and Franklin and Magdeline Diaz were visiting from Lawrenceville, Georgia, while Adriana Diaz lives in the Dominican Republic, according to police. They were among a group of six pedestrians that were walking back to their parked car after leaving the Marlins game when the crash occurred at about 7:30 Saturday night.
It appeared the driver might have had a heart attack or some type of medical condition when he lost control, witnesses said.
Police said they continue to investigate.

article.title: Driver involved in crash that killed UA student, Calera woman was off-duty deputy
article.text:
Driver involved in crash that killed UA student, Calera woman was off-duty deputy
ESCAMBIA COUNTY, Fla. — A tragic incident early Saturday morning claimed the lives of two pedestrians, including a University of Alabama student, on Perdido Key Drive.
The Florida Highway Patrol reported that a 47-year-old Pensacola man was driving east on State Road 292 when he struck two individuals crossing the road just after 3 a.m.
The driver of the vehicle involved has been identified as an off-duty Escambia County Deputy in an unmarked Escambia County Sheriff's Office patrol car, according to the Florida Highway Patrol (FHP).
FHP says the deputy had been returning home from an off-duty job at the time of the crash. The deputy has been placed on administrative leave, per policy.
"They did not activate the crosswalk lights. They went across dark," Commissioner Steve Stroberger said. "And I think there was an EMS vehicle that was actually en route to another call and they actually witnessed it. And they noticed that it hadn't been activated." He noted the dark crosswalks are hard to see. Stroberger explained some lights go out before you finish crossing. He is discussing safety enhancements with FDOT.
Sources with The University of Alabama confirmed that Jackson Cunningham, a University of Alabama student and member of the Theta Chi fraternity, is one of the people killed. The other victim is 20-year-old Nicole Moore from Calera according to reports by WEAR-TV. Both were pronounced dead at the scene.
Theta Chi Alabama shared on social media that Cunningham, a native of Hoover, Alabama, was a member of the pledge class of 2022. The post described him as "a man of unwavering character, faith, and kindness," adding that "his impact on the lives of others will last forever."
A gofundme has been set up to help the family with funeral expenses.
Paul Mitchell the School in Birmingham posted a tribute to Moore:
Our hearts are heavy as we remember Nicole Moore, one of our bright and beautiful future professionals.
Nicole brought laughter, light, and warmth to every room she walked into. Her sense of humor, talent, and kindness left a lasting impression on everyone students and staff alike.
She will be deeply missed, forever loved, and always remembered.
Rest in peace, Nicole.
article.author: Gracie Johnson; Cynthia Gould

article.title: Drivers killed after wrong-way head-on crash in Polk County, deputies say
article.text:
FROSTPROOF, Fla. — A wrong-way head-on crash late Thursday night on Highway 27 resulted in the deaths of both drivers.
That crash happened around 10 p.m. in the southbound lane in unincorporated Frostproof, the Polk County Sheriff's Office said in a news release.
According to evidence collected at the scene and a witness, a 2021 black Toyota Corolla was heading north in the left southbound lane while a red Nissan Altima was heading south in the left southbound lane.
"The two vehicles collided in a head-on crash, and the Toyota immediately caught fire," deputies said in the release.
The driver of that car, a 41-year-old Sebring woman, was declared dead at the scene. Meanwhile, the driver of the Nissan, a 32-year-old Kissimmee man, was ejected from his car and declared dead soon after. His passenger, a 31-year-old woman who deputies say was either his spouse or girlfriend, suffered critical injuries in the crash.
That woman was airlifted as a trauma alert to a local hospital, where deputies say she remains in critical but stable condition.
"A 911 call was received at the same time as the crash occurred, reporting a car traveling the wrong way (north in the southbound lanes) at a high rate of speed on US 27 in that area," deputies said.
article.author: Patrick Henkels

article.title: Tampa man hit, killed by SUV while crossing Hillsborough Avenue: FHP
article.text:
TAMPA, Fla. - A 75-year-old man is dead after he was hit by an SUV while crossing West Hillsborough Avenue in Tampa Thursday evening, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
MORE NEWS: Hillsborough driver involved in deadly DUI crash found guilty of lesser charge
The backstory:
At around 6:30 p.m., a Ford SUV, driven by a 72-year-old Tampa man, was traveling eastbound on Hillsborough Avenue near Town N Country Boulevard when a pedestrian tried to cross the road in front of oncoming traffic, FHP said.
Troopers say the pedestrian was struck by the SUV and taken to a local hospital, where he passed away from his injuries.
The Source: Information for this story was provided by the Florida Highway Patrol.

According to FHP, at 11:56 p.m. Nov. 1, 2024, Michael David Davis, of Eustis, was driving a 2017 Dodge Ram 2500 the wrong way in the eastbound lane of State Road 44 when he struck a 2024 Harley-Davidson Sportster head-on near Hammond Lane. The 20-year-old rider, of Umatilla, was pronounced dead at the scene. A second motorcyclist, a 41-year-old Umatilla man riding a 2003 Suzuki VL800K, […]

A 60-year-old Lady Lake man riding a Harley-Davidson motorcycle westbound on Mondon Hill Road struck the Toyota. Both men were transported to area hospitals with serious injuries. The motorcyclist later died, FHP said.

Hayes deceased in the roadway near the intersection of U.S. Highway 27 and English Road, Lt. Shannon Walsh said. The driver fled the scene. “The suspect vehicle is believed to have front-end

Around 5:30 p.m., a 59-year-old Umatilla man was driving a 2016 Ford F-150 northbound on Calhoun Road when he attempted to make a left turn onto Kine Drive, FHP Lt. Tara Crescenzi said. The Ford turned into the direct path of a motorcycle being ridden by a man who had not been identified at press time. Around 2:10 p.m., a 69-year-old New Boston, Michigan man was driving a 2023 Ford Explorer northbound on County Road 473 near Allyson Road when the SUV ran off the roadway for an unknown reason and struck a tree, FHP said. The driver was airlifted to Orlando Regional Medical Center with serious injuries. His passenger, a 69-year-old woman, also of New Boston, Michigan, was transported to AdventHealth Waterman in Tavares, where she was pronounced
GROVELAND, Fla. — The Florida Highway Patrol is investigating a crash that killed two Mascotte teens early Sunday morning. A 19-year-old Mascotte man was driving a 2018 Dodge Challenger southbound on County Road 565 in the area of Sloans Ridge Road near Groveland when he lost control, troopers said. The Dodge left the roadway, struck multiple trees and became engulfed in flames around 12:45 a.m. The driver and his passenger, a 17-year-old Mascotte boy, were both killed, FHP said. The crash was the second fatal crash involving fire in Lake County in just a few hours. Around 8 p.m. Saturday,
SORRENTO, Fla. — A Tavares man was killed Saturday night when a large truck struck the motorcycle he was riding head-on, throwing him into the path of another vehicle, according to the Florida Highway Patrol. Shortly before 8 p.m., a 27-year-old Hobe Sound man was driving a 2008 Ford F-650 eastbound on State Road 44 near Huff Road when he crossed into the westbound lane for an unknown reason, FHP said. The Ford hit a 2023 Indian Roadmaster motorcycle head-on, causing it to burst into flames and throwing the rider, a 39-year-old Tavares man, into the direct path of a
LADY LAKE, Fla. — A pedestrian walking on Rolling Acres Road was killed Monday after being struck by a vehicle, according to the Florida Highway Patrol. The 25-year-old Lady Lake man was walking south near Abeble Road shortly before 12:45 p.m. when he was hit by a 2008 Ford Edge being driven southbound by an 18-year-old Lady Lake woman, FHP said. The impact caused the man to collide with a fence, and he was pronounced deceased on the scene. The woman was not injured.
CLERMONT, Fla. — A man was killed after a construction steamroller overturned and struck him, according to the Florida Highway Patrol. The 35-year-old Ocala man was operating the steamroller on private property near County Road 455, west of Hancock Road, around 11:30 a.m. Monday, FHP said. He was attempting to drive the steamroller up an embankment when it overturned. He was struck during the rollover and suffered fatal injuries, FHP said. The incident remains under investigation.
ORANGE COUNTY, Fla.—A Groveland man has died after crashing a motorcycle into a guardrail early Saturday, according to the Florida Highway Patrol. The 45-year-old man was riding a 2021 Harley-Davidson Road Glide westbound in the outside lane of State Road 408 near State Road 50 when veered ff the road for an unknown reason and struck a guardrail, shortly before 1:30 a.m., FHP said. The man was transported to AdventHealth East and pronounced deceased. The crash remains under investigation.
CLERMONT, Fla. — The Florida Highway Patrol is investigating a fiery crash that killed one person early Sunday morning. At 2:45 a.m., a 2008 Mazda 3 was traveling westbound on County Road 474 east of Bruno Road in unincorporated Clermont when, for unknown reasons, it ran off the roadway, overturned and became engulfed in flames, according to FHP. The driver, who has not been identified, was pronounced dead at the scene. Troopers are awaiting identification from the medical examiner.
EUSTIS, Fla. — Police have released the identity of the woman killed on Lakeshore Drive Friday morning. Tracy Schievenin, 48, was riding a bicycle southbound on Lakeshore Drive, approaching the intersection of Eustis Road when she was struck from behind by an unknown vehicle, according to Eustis Police Department. The unknown vehicle fled the scene and Schievenin was later found deceased, EPD said. The investigation closed a portion of Lakeshore Drive for nearly seven hours. Lakeshore Drive is closed between Eustis Road and Woodward Avenue as authorities investigate after a pedestrian was found deceased Friday morning, Capri told Inside Lake. This is a developing story.

TAVARES, Fla.— Tavares Police Department is investigating a crash that killed a man Sunday. Blake Sawyer, 21, of Tavares, was riding a dirt bike on Dead River Road shortly before 12:15 p.m. when he attempted to pass vehicles using the shoulder, according to a preliminary investigation, Tavares Police Department Public Information Officer Courtney Sullivan said. Sawyer struck a pole and crashed. He was wearing a helmet, Sullivan said. Sawyer was transported to an area hospital, where he was pronounced deceased. The crash remains under investigation.
MONTVERDE, Fla.—A 39-year-old Howey-in-the-Hills man was thrown from his motorcycle and killed Sunday afternoon when he ran off the road, according to the Florida Highway Patrol. Shortly before 5:15 p.m., the man was riding a 2007 Suzuki GSX-R600 northbound on County Road 455, approaching Woodlands Drive when he failed to negotiate a curve and ran off the road, FHP said. He was thrown from the motorcycle and pronounced deceased on the scene. He was wearing a helmet, FHP said.
SUMTER COUNTY, Fla.—The Florida Highway Patrol is investigating a golf cart crash that killed two people Friday night. The golf cart collided with a Kia Soul that was traveling southbound on U.S. Highway 27 and overturned. The golf cart driver, and his passenger, a 58-year-old woman of The Villages, were transported to a local hospital, where they later succumbed to their injuries, according to FHP.

The 67-year-old man of The Villages was riding a Kawasaki Ninja 500 eastbound on Woodridge Drive when he lost control and ran off the road, shortly after 6 p.m., FHP said. The motorcycle hit a tree, and the man was pronounced deceased on the scene.

Shortly after 5:45 a.m., the 57-year-old man from Leesburg was driving a 2005 Ford F-150 southbound on County Road 473, near Lake Eustis Drive, when he struck the left side of the trailer of a 2016 semi-truck that was blocking the roadway while backing into a private driveway, according to the Florida Highway Patrol. A passenger in the F-150 was transported to Orlando Regional Medical Center with non-life-threatening injuries. The occupants of the semi-truck, a 67-year-old man and a 63-year-old man, both from Arkansas, were not injured.

Troopers said the man may have suffered a medical episode prior to the crash. He was transported to Orlando Health South Lake Hospital, where he was pronounced deceased. The GMC overturned several times, ejecting the woman, FHP said. She was pronounced deceased on the scene. The 18-year-old woman was driving a 2023 Ford Mustang Mach-E southbound, north of mile marker 291, when she hit the back of a 2024 Hino tow truck that was merging from the outside shoulder into the outside lane, around 9:45 p.m., FHP said. The impact killed the Southwest Ranches woman; the tow truck driver, a 53-year-old Ocala man, was not injured. The crash closed State Road 44 and it was still closed nearly three hours after the crash.

Ken Toler said. Mangual was pronounced deceased on the scene, the driver of the truck, Anthony Campbell, 55, of Astatula was not injured. OCALA NATIONAL FOREST—A Sorrento man has died after crashing a dirt bike Sunday, according to the Florida Highway Patrol. Around 11:10 a.m., the 59-year-old man was riding a 2022 KTM Dirt Bike on a dirt road in the Ocala National Forest when he crashed into two trees and was thrown from the bike, FHP said. article.title: Titusville man, 23, killed after crashing motorcycle while going around curve: police
article.text:
MELBOURNE, Fla. - A Titusville man was killed in a motorcycle crash in Brevard County on Tuesday night, police said.
The Melbourne Police Department identified the victim as 23-year-old Trevor Dean Hackbardt.
Officers responded to a crash at around 9:45 p.m. at the intersection of North Harbor City Boulevard and Babcock Street. Upon arrival, they found Hackbardt suffering from life-threatening injuries.
He was taken to a local hospital, where he was later pronounced dead.
An initial investigation revealed that Hackbardt was traveling north on North Harbor City Boulevard when he lost control while going around a curve and struck a utility pole.
Police said there is no indication that alcohol or drugs were factors in the crash.
The crash remains under investigation. Anyone with information is asked to call the traffic enforcement unit at 321-616-6027.

article.title: Man driving pickup in Marion bus crash charged with 8 counts of DUI manslaughter
article.text:
MARION COUNTY, Fla. — Eight people died and 38 others were injured when a bus carrying farm workers collided with a pickup truck on State Road 40 in Marion County. Read live updates below:
10:10 p.m. update
The Mexican Consulate said the farmworkers on the bus were as young as 18, with workers as old as in their 40s.
The organization said that one victim, a man in his early 30s, leaves behind a 1-year-old daughter. Family members said he planned to work for five years and provide a better future for his daughter.
The Consulate is trying to speak with the Olvera Trucking Corp and Cannon Farms in Dunnellon. As of Tuesday evening, they have not made contact with either.
The Farmworker Association created a GoFundMe for the victims and their families.
The funds raised will support those affected by the tragic accident.
Read more here.
8:20 p.m. update
WFTV has received a copy of the U.S. Department of Labor form from Olvera Tucking requesting 43 workers to pick watermelon from May 1 to June 4.
See below to read a copy of the H-2A Agricultural Clearance Order - Form ETA-790A:
7:20 p.m. update
Florida Department of Transportation officials said State Road 40 between Ocala and Dunnellon has reopened to traffic.
The bus crash had closed the road for over 12 hours.
6:25 p.m. update
The Florida Highway State Patrol said it could be up to six months before the facts come back surrounding the tragic death of eight migrant workers who were on the way to a watermelon farm in Marion County.
“I’ve worked many fatalities in my career, and this is the death that you do not expect,” Marion County Sheriff Billy Woods said Tuesday.
Officials said a retired school bus was carrying 53 migrant workers Tuesday morning when a Ford pickup truck collided with the bus on State Road 40.
FHP said the bus was traveling west on the highway when the pickup made its way to the center lane and sideswiped the bus.
The bus veered onto the shoulder of the roadway, hit a tree and went through a fence before overturning in an open pasture.
On Tuesday, troopers arrested Bryan Maclean Howard, the driver of the pickup, on eight counts of driving under the influence manslaughter.
Residents who live nearby said the roadway is notorious for crashes.
“It’s not uncommon to have this road closed down because of fatalities,” said Ashley Smith, a Marion County resident. “A lot of single fatalities, but not of this magnitude. Eight people in one morning is a record.”
Investigators are still working to confirm the identity of the victims.
FHP said a commercial motor vehicle team was at the scene to specifically investigate the bus involved in the crash.
Watch live team coverage of the crash on Channel 9 Eyewitness News.
5 p.m. update
The Florida Highway Patrol said Tuesday afternoon that it has made an arrest in a bus crash that left eight people dead and 38 others injured.
The agency said that shortly after 1:45 p.m., it arrested Bryan Maclean Howard -- the driver of a 2001 Ford Ranger pickup truck involved in the crash -- on eight counts of driving under the influence manslaughter.
Investigators said the crash involved Howard’s pickup and a 2010 International Bus that was transporting about 53 employees of a farming company.
The agency said the vehicles were involved in a “sideswipe-type collision,” causing the bus to travel off the roadway, through a fence and then overturn.
FHP said it will release the identities of those killed in the crash once the victims’ relatives have been notified of their deaths.
“Our sympathies and prayers are with the families of the deceased,” the agency said in a statement. “Consistent with our duties, the Florida Highway Patrol will conduct both a thorough and exhaustive traffic crash and criminal investigation.”
3:55 p.m. update
AdventHealth Ocala said it has transferred two of the 16 patients brought to its hospital to other facilities.
The hospital said that the remaining 14 patients in its care are in the process of being discharged.
Watch live team coverage on the crash on Channel 9 Eyewitness News, beginning at 4 p.m.
Click here to stream live news.
2:45 p.m. update
AdventHealth Ocala will hold a live news conference at 3 p.m.
You can watch it live on Channel 9 or by clicking here.
2:25 p.m. update
Orlando’s Mexican Consulate seeks information about victims in Tuesday’s deadly bus crash in Marion County.
Consulmex Orlando said it is trying to make contact with those affected by the crash, which involved farmworkers.
Those who might know the victims or have information about them are asked to call the Consulate at 520-623-7864.
Click here to read more.
12:45 p.m. update
Marion County Fire Rescue said firefighters arrived at the crash scene within nine minutes of being dispatched.
They said firefighters discovered a bus that had rolled over and immediately called for more help.
Firefighters said they counted a total of 46 patients, with eight of them being declared dead at the scene by paramedics.
They said 38 people were taken to hospitals, with eight in critical condition and 30 with non-life-threatening injuries.
“It is with heavy hearts that we confirm the loss of eight lives,” Marion County fire Chief James Banta said. “I would like to extend my deepest gratitude to the 35 MCFR units that promptly responded to the emergency. I also want to thank our partners at the Marion County Sheriff’s Office, Emergency Management, HCA Ocala, AdventHealth Ocala and the Marion County Public School system for their invaluable assistance in transporting 45 patients to local hospitals. Their coordinated efforts were crucial in managing this crisis.”
Watch Channel 9 Eyewitness News to see live team coverage on the crash.
11:45 a.m. update
AdventHealth told Channel 9 that it is currently treating 16 patients -- 12 at AdventHealth Ocala and four at AdventHealth Timber Ridge ER.
“Our prayers are with all those affected by the tragic bus accident in Ocala this morning,” said Dr. Rodrigo Torres, AdventHealth Ocala’s chief medical officer. “Our immediate priority is to provide exceptional medical care to those injured and compassionate support to our patients and their loved ones.”
Marion County officials will provide a live update momentarily. Watch it live on Channel 9 or by clicking here.
10:50 a.m. update
The Florida Highway Patrol said the crash happened at 6:35 a.m. on State Road 40 -- about 500 feet west of Southwest 148 Court.
Troopers said the collision involved a 2010 International Bus and a 2001 Ford Ranger pickup truck.
FHP said 53 farm workers were aboard the bus.
“For reasons to be determined, the two vehicles collided in a sideswiped manner,” an FHP spokesman said. “Post collision, the bus traveled off the roadway through a fence and then overturned.”
Troopers said eight people have died in the crash and approximately 40 others have been taken to hospitals for treatment.
Watch Channel 9 Eyewitness News at Noon for live team coverage on the crash.
See a map of the crash scene below:
Click here to download our free news, weather and smart TV apps. And click here to stream Channel 9 Eyewitness News live.
10:40 a.m. update
Marion County Fire Rescue now says an eighth person has died in a bus crash early Tuesday.
It said 53 others were injured in the crash, and they were taken to a hospital for treatment.
The crash happened shortly before 6:45 a.m. on State Road 40 near Southwest 157th Terrace.
10:25 a.m. update
At least seven people died in a bus crash early Tuesday in Marion County, the Florida Highway Patrol said.
The crash happened shortly before 6:45 a.m. on State Road 40 near Southwest 157th Terrace.
Troopers said some of the people died at the scene and others died at a hospital.
They said the crash involved at least two vehicles, including a bus.
The road is closed in the area.
Earlier story:
Florida Highway Patrol is investigating a deadly crash Tuesday morning in Marion County.
Troopers said the crash happened around 6:43 a.m. on State Road 40 near SW 157th Terrace.
Marion County officials said the crash involved a “large-capacity vehicle.”
Deputies in Marion County are calling the incident a “serious accident with fatalities.”
Marion County Public Schools said a school bus was sent to the scene to help transport victims from the crash.
School officials confirmed the crash did not involve a school bus, and no students were hurt.
Troopers are investigating the cause of the crash.
SR 40 is shut down in both directions and drivers need to seek alternate routes.
Channel 9 has a crew working to gather more details and will provide updates on Eyewitness News.
article.author: Jason Kelly

article.title: Head-on crash kills Bell man, injures 5 Gainesville people on State Road 121
article.text:
A 26-year-old Bell man died and five Gainesville people were injured in a head-on collision on Saturday afternoon north of Gainesville on State Road 121 in Alachua County.
According to a Florida Highway Patrol (FHP) release, the Bell man was driving a pickup and a 21-year-old Gainesville man was driving a van when the vehicles – going in opposite directions on SR 121 near NW 144th Avenue at 1:05 p.m. – collided head-on.
Emergency responders pronounced the Bell man dead on scene and transported the five Gainesville people in the van to UF Health Shands Hospital.
The Gainesville driver was reportedly in critical condition and the four female passengers (no ages given) were all listed in serious condition.
SR 121 was closed for several hours on Saturday as FHP conducted the investigation.

article.title: Miami Motorcycle Accident News: Investigations into Double-Fatal Broward I-95 Crash Underway
article.text:
MIAMI, FL— August 1, 2011 – Authorities are investigating a violent Broward County motorcycle crash that resulted in two fatalities on the morning of July 29, 2011. According to information provided by the South Florida Sun-Sentinel, the fatal accident occurred when the motorcycle careened off an elevated Interstate 95 exit ramp for unknown reasons.
Reports indicated 24-year-old Luis Gustavo was operating his motorcycle on I-95 when it traveled off the elevated exit ramp near the I-95/I-595 interchange, subsequently crashing onto the CSX Railroad tracks.
Sgt. Mark Wysocky of the Florida Highway Patrol (FHP) said the Broward Sheriff’s Office (BSO) was notified of the deadly motorcycle accident at approximately 6:40 a.m., when CSX operators contacted them to report finding the bodies of two crash victims on the train tracks. A passenger who was riding on the motorcycle when it crashed, whom authorities identified as 20-year-old Stephanie A. Fredericks, was also killed in the I-95 accident.
Data collected by Sun-Sentinel reporters suggested there had been at least seven accidents in which motorcyclists tumbled from elevated highway exit ramps in the area over the last decade. Sgt. Wysocky noted that speed was deemed to be a factor in the majority of those wrecks. A full probe into the fatal I-95 wreck was expected to be underway.
According to statistics provided by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA), a total of 4,462 motorcycle deaths were recorded throughout the nation during the year 2009. Florida crashes resulted in 413 of those motorcycle fatalities. Overall, statewide traffic crashes claimed the lives of 2,558 people.

Life in large metropolitan areas is primarily characterized by a fast-pace lifestyle, which includes crowded streets. People depend on automobile transportation daily for their professional and personal travels. Unfortunately, accidents are inevitable. South Florida auto accident lawyer Francisco J. Viñas has successfully litigated and settled a multitude of auto accident claims involving cars, trucks, tractor trailers, motorcycles and bicycles. With over 15 years of experience, Miami accident attorney Francisco Frank J. Viñas is committed to representing individuals of personal injury cases in their fight for justice and rightful compensation.
