article.title: Motorcyclist Critically Injured After Sedan Turns Into His Path on SR 64 in Manatee County - Justice Pays
article.text:
Updated: November 10, 2025 — Bradenton, FL – A serious crash at River Heritage Boulevard and State Road 64 in Manatee County left a motorcyclist critically injured Sunday afternoon. Authorities say a turning car entered the rider’s path, raising renewed concern about intersection safety on this busy stretch of SR 64.
How the Crash at SR 64 and River Heritage Happened
The crash took place around 4:30 p.m. on Sunday, November 9, 2025, at the intersection of State Road 64 and River Heritage Boulevard in eastern Manatee County.
According to the Florida Highway Patrol, a 75-year-old woman from Bradenton was driving a Toyota Corolla westbound on SR 64 with her 73-year-old male passenger, also from Bradenton. At the same time, a 28-year-old man from Parrish was riding his motorcycle eastbound on SR 64, approaching the same intersection.
As the vehicles neared River Heritage Boulevard, the driver of the Toyota moved into the left-turn lane to enter the neighborhood. When she began turning, she pulled directly into the motorcyclist’s path. The rider tried to brake and swerve, but there was no time to avoid the impact.
The motorcycle struck the right side of the car, throwing the rider onto the roadway. Witnesses reported a violent collision that blocked multiple lanes of traffic.
Emergency Response and Injuries Reported
First responders from Manatee County EMS and the Florida Highway Patrol arrived quickly at the scene. The 28-year-old rider was airlifted by Bay-Flight to Blake Hospital in critical condition. The driver and passenger of the Toyota suffered minor injuries and remained at the scene to assist investigators.
Troopers temporarily closed portions of State Road 64 as crash reconstruction teams documented evidence and cleared debris. The FHP Traffic Homicide Unit is continuing its investigation to determine contributing factors and whether any citations will be issued.
This serious accident once again highlights the dangers faced by motorcycle riders and the importance of extra caution from drivers making turns at busy intersections throughout Manatee County.
Editor’s note: Details of this crash were sourced from the official Florida Highway Patrol report. Minor clarifications were made for readability and consistency with FHP’s intended narrative, as the original report contained typographical errors regarding vehicle direction.
article.author: Danny Murphy

article.title: Driver arrested for Highlands County crash that killed 13-year-old boy, injured 2 others
article.text:
LAKE PLACID, Fla. - An 18-year-old involved in a deadly crash while speeding from the Florida Highway Patrol in Highlands County has been arrested.
The backstory:
On January 21, 2025, the Florida Highway Patrol tried to stop a 2021 Kia Forte for speeding on US 27 near Lake Henry Drive in Highlands County.
Troopers say instead of stopping, the Kia sped away.
While speeding from FHP, troopers say the Kia collided with the back of a 2012 Ford E350.
CLICK HERE:>>> Follow FOX 13 on YouTube
Troopers say a 13-year-old boy in the Kia was taken to an area hospital with critical injuries and later died.
Deanthony Torres booking photo courtesy of the St. Lucie County Sheriff's Office.
Two other passengers in the Kia, a 16-year-old boy and a 16-year-old girl, were taken to an area hospital with injuries.
The driver of the Kia, who has since been identified as Deanthony Torres, 18, of Okeechobee, was taken to an area hospital. He was released on Wednesday and booked into the St. Lucie County Jail.
The driver of the Ford E350 was not injured in the crash.
What's next:
Torres is awaiting extradition back to Highlands County.
Torres has been charged with aggravated manslaughter of a child, aggravated fleeing to elude with serious injury or death, driving with a suspended license (serious injury or death) and two counts of attempted murder.
The Source: This story was written with information provided by the Florida Highway Patrol.

article.title: 5 hospitalized following I-75 crash in Pasco County
article.text:
WESLEY CHAPEL, Fla. - Pasco County fire rescue and the Florida Highway Patrol responded to a multi-vehicle crash on Interstate 75 that left three people seriously injured.
The three-vehicle crash occurred around 11 a.m. Thursday just south of Overpass Road, which is about 3.5 miles south of State Road 52.
According to FHP, two of the vehicles slowed down for traffic, but the driver of a pickup truck, who was distracted by spilled coffee, did not slow down. The pickup collided with a sedan, which then hit a third vehicle.
A total of five people were taken to a nearby hospital, three of whom had to be airlifted due to the severity of their injuries. All of the injured people were occupants of the sedan that had been hit by the pickup truck. Two teenage boys, ages 13 and 14, were critically injured in the crash, and a 33-year-old male passenger of the sedan was seriously injured. Two others in the car, including the driver, suffered minor injuries.
The 33-year-old driver of the pickup truck was not injured.

article.title: Lanes Reopen After Fatal Collision On Tamiami Trail In Nokomis - Tampa Free Press
article.text:
NOKOMIS, Fla. – Traffic is moving again in Nokomis following a fatal morning crash at the intersection of US 41 and Bayshore Road. Sarasota County Sheriff’s deputies and the Florida Highway Patrol have cleared the scene after an investigation into the single-fatality accident that disrupted travel for several hours.
The crash originally forced the closure of both the northbound and southbound fast lanes on North Tamiami Trail. During the investigation, law enforcement advised that “citizens traveling in the area should expect traffic delays” as troopers worked to document the scene.
As of this update, the Florida Highway Patrol has concluded its initial on-site work, and all lanes are now open to through traffic. While the roadway is clear, officials have not yet released specific details regarding the cause of the accident or the identity of the deceased.
ORIGINAL REPORT: A fatal traffic accident at the intersection of US 41 and Bayshore Road has prompted an emergency response and significant traffic delays in Nokomis. Sarasota County Sheriff’s deputies are currently on the scene assisting the Florida Highway Patrol with an investigation into the crash, which resulted in one confirmed death.
The collision has forced authorities to close both the northbound and southbound fast lanes of North Tamiami Trail. These closures remain in effect while troopers conduct a forensic reconstruction of the scene and clear debris.
Law enforcement officials have issued a public notice stating that “citizens traveling in the area should expect traffic delays” for the foreseeable future. There is currently no estimated time for when the lanes will reopen to normal traffic.
Drivers are being encouraged to seek alternate routes or allow for extra travel time while the Florida Highway Patrol continues its work at the intersection.
No further details regarding the identities of those involved or the cause of the crash have been released at this time.

If everybodys not out the door in 20 minutes everybodys getting shot, a caller told a 911 dispatcher. The man stayed in the office for a few minutes before walking away toward Westmoreland and Colonial Drive where officers eventually detained him.
Citizens Review Board rejects findings in fatal Orlando police shooting
Read full article: Citizens Review Board rejects findings in fatal Orlando police shootingORLANDO, Fla. The Citizens Review Board has rejected an internal investigation that exonerated an Orlando officer who fatally shot a man at a Colonial Drive shopping plaza in 2018. During a meeting on Wednesday, board members voted 7-1 to disapprove the findings of Internal Affairs investigators with the Orlando Police Department. Both officers were eventually cleared of wrongdoing by the Florida Department of Law Enforcement and an internal investigation by OPD. At Wednesday's meeting, board members heard from an attorney representing the Silva family. We just need to follow the policy as written, board member Tom Keen said.
Fatal crash closes stretch of Colonial Drive in Orlando
Read full article: Fatal crash closes stretch of Colonial Drive in OrlandoORLANDO, Fla. A fatal crash Monday morning has forced Orlando police to close a busy stretch of Colonial Drive. The wreck was reported at Hyer Avenue and Colonial Drive, which is closed from Mills Avenue to Summerlin Avenue. Orlando police said the traffic lights are out Mills Avenue from Amelia Street to Marks Street. We are asking citizens to find an alternate route at this time, police said in an email. Details about the crash have not been released.
Motorcyclist killed in Orange County crash
Read full article: Motorcyclist killed in Orange County crashORLANDO, Fla. A motorcyclist was killed in a crash early Friday in Orange County, officials said. The fatal crash was reported on Colonial Drive at Dean Road. Details about the wreck have not been released, but family members told News 6 the victim just turned 25 years old. #BREAKING: FHP investigating a fatal motorcycle crash at Colonial & Lake Downey. @news6wkmg pic.twitter.com/sv53BMITzM Justin Campbell (@JCampbellNews6) July 10, 2020Six people were detained after the crash, but they were all released, officials said.
Several hurt in Orange County crash
Read full article: Several hurt in Orange County crashORLANDO, Fla. An early morning crash involving as many as four cars in Orange County sent several people to the hospital. The multi-car wreck happened just before 2 a.m. at Colonial Drive and Hiawassee Road. A passenger in one of the cars said another driver ran a red light, but details about the crash and the exact number of victims have not been released. Video from the scene showed several damaged vehicles, including a car that had its roof torn off.
Traffic alert: I-4 Ultimate opening Colonial ramps in final alignments
Read full article: Traffic alert: I-4 Ultimate opening Colonial ramps in final alignmentsORLANDO, Fla. Beginning Friday, the Florida Department of Transportations I-4 Ultimate project will reach another milestone with the scheduled opening of the westbound I-4 exit ramp to Colonial Drive, Exit 84, and the westbound I-4 on ramp from Colonial Drive in their final alignments, according to a news release. Colonial Drive under I-4 closes nightly from 10 p.m. to 5 a.m. until June 26. pic.twitter.com/5trm5X4AkS I-4 Ultimate and Beyond (@I4Ultimate) June 24, 2020The ramps will open to traffic as soon as 5 a.m., Friday, June 26. Westbound I-4 also shifts onto brand new travel lanes over Colonial Drive, FDOT officials said in a news release. Ivanhoe Boulevard motorists will drive across Colonial Drive before entering westbound I-4, according to officials.
Driver dies after being ejected in rollover crash, troopers say
Read full article: Driver dies after being ejected in rollover crash, troopers sayORLANDO, Fla. One person is dead following a two-vehicle crash along Colonial Drive in Orlando Wednesday, according to the Florida Highway Patrol. Troopers responded to the area of Colonial Drive and Avalon Park Boulevard just before 2 a.m. for the report of a vehicle crash. Following the crash, the pickup truck traveled into the shoulder of the road and overturned, ejecting the driver, according to a FHP report. The driver of the pickup truck, a 22-year-old male from Chulutoa, was taken to the hospital where he later died. It was not immediately clear what led up to the crash, and troopers say it remains under investigation.
FDOT to close I-4 entrance ramp from Orange Blossom Trail
Read full article: FDOT to close I-4 entrance ramp from Orange Blossom TrailORLANDO, Fla. – The Florida Department of Transportation will be closing the westbound I-4 entrance ramp from southbound Orange Blossom Trail as soon as Monday. Officials said trucks will follow Orange Blossom Trail to Colonial Drive and John Young Parkway to enter I-4. Accessing westbound I-4 from northbound Orange Blossom Trail:Continue north onto Orange Blossom Trail. Turn left onto John Young Parkway. Accessing westbound I-4 from southbound Orange Blossom Trail:Turn right onto Colonial Drive.
Another round of coronavirus testing coming to Barnett Park next week
Read full article: Another round of coronavirus testing coming to Barnett Park next weekORANGE COUNTY, Fla. Barnett Park will once again be transformed into a coronavirus testing site for most of next week. The Florida Department of Health in Orange County announced Thursday that testing will take place at the Colonial Drive park from June 15 to June 18. Appointments are required and anyone who gets tested will receive free face masks and hand sanitizer. To find other testing sites around Orange County, go to OCFL.net/COVID19. To keep up with the latest news on the pandemic, subscribe to News 6s coronavirus newsletter or go to ClickOrlando.com/coronavirus.
Man charged in January double-fatal crash on Colonial Drive
Read full article: Man charged in January double-fatal crash on Colonial DriveORLANDO, Fla. – The man Orlando police say caused a wreck on Colonial Drive in January that killed two and injured two others was arrested Thursday on charges of vehicular homicide, according to the department. The double-fatal crash happened on Jan. 9 around 2:40 a.m. on Colonial Drive at Cathcart Avenue. The crash involved a Volkswagen Jetta and Ford F-150 pickup truck. After the wreck, Orlando Police Sgt. According to police, K-9 units and a helicopter were called to the area because the pickup truck, found unoccupied, ended up on the edge of Park Lake.
Motorcyclist killed in Colonial Drive crash, troopers say
Read full article: Motorcyclist killed in Colonial Drive crash, troopers sayORANGE COUNTY, Fla. – A motorcyclist was killed Thursday in a wreck on Colonial Drive, according to the Florida Highway Patrol. FHP troopers responded to the crash at Colonial Drive and Sherman Street at 5:05 pm. A motorcycle and truck were involved, troopers said. The motorcyclist, who has not been identified, was pronounced deceased on scene. The eastbound lanes of Colonial Drive were blocked Thursday evening as investigators were still on scene.
Colonial Drive reopens in Orlando after fatal crash
Read full article: Colonial Drive reopens in Orlando after fatal crashPublished: March 2, 2020, 5:50 am Updated: March 2, 2020, 8:14 amORLANDO, Fla. – A fatal crash early Monday prompted Orlando police to close a stretch of Colonial Drive. The wreck was reported on West Colonial Drive near Edgewater Drive. Colonial Drive was closed westbound at Parramore Avenue and eastbound at Westmoreland Drive, but the roadway was reopened around 8 a.m. Details about the crash have not been released.
Orange County shooting leaves man injured
Read full article: Orange County shooting leaves man injuredORANGE COUNTY, Fla. – A 49-year-old man was shot early Thursday in Orange County, deputies said. The Orange County Sheriff’s Office said deputies were called around 2 a.m. to the 5500 block of West Colonial Drive, where a man said he had been shot by another man. The victim, who suffered non-life-threatening injuries, was taken to Orlando Regional Medical Center, according to deputies. The victim said the shooting happened near Balboa Drive and Governors Avenue. Deputies, however, said there was evidence of a shooting in that area.
I-4 construction to close busy Orlando on-ramp for six months
Read full article: I-4 construction to close busy Orlando on-ramp for six monthsORLANDO, Fla. – Construction crews are closing a busy Orlando Interstate 4 on-ramp for at least six months to build new lanes for the I-4 Ultimate Project. Starting as soon as 10 p.m. Sunday, the I-4 westbound entrance ramp from Colonial Drive will be shut down. After the ramp closes, drivers will be directed south on Hughey Avenue for about a mile to the westbound on-ramp at South Street. The WB I-4 entrance ramp from Colonial Dr. is scheduled to close for six months as soon as 10 p.m. Sunday, Feb. 2. In addition to the closure, Colonial Drive under I-4 will also close between 10 p.m. and 5 a.m. Feb. 2.
Kidnapping suspect shot by Orlando police officer, chief says
Read full article: Kidnapping suspect shot by Orlando police officer, chief saysORLANDO, Fla. – An Orlando police officer shot a kidnapping suspect at a shopping center on West Colonial Drive Wednesday morning, Orlando police Chief Orlando Rolón said. Police said the shooting happened at 2124 West Colonial Drive in the Western Way Shopping Center parking lot. Investigators are interviewing the second suspect in the vehicle involved in the kidnapping and the kidnapping victim, who were both unharmed. The Orlando Police Department later identified the officer who shot Billings as Officer Kyle Medvetz, who was hired by the department in 2013. De'Angelo Stephenson (Orange County Jail)Rolón said the sergeant who saw the kidnapping was not the officer who shot the suspect.
Orange County house fire kills cat and leaves 1 patient with smoke inhalation
Read full article: Orange County house fire kills cat and leaves 1 patient with smoke inhalationOrange County, Fla. – Orange County officials are at the scene of a house fire near Colonial Drive Thursday. According to officials, the one-alarm fire happened at 536 Hampton Avenue around 8:51 a.m.ON SCENE: @OrlandoFireDept working a house fire along Hampton Avenue. GOOD NEWS: After a secondary search, a second cat was found alive and is now being treated for smoke inhalation. Fire officials told News 6 that the fire is contained, but still smoking. Full Screen 1 / 6 House fire at 536 Hampton AvenueThis is the second fire this week that killed a pet inside a home.
Traffic alert: Big change comes to Colonial Drive exit on I-4
Read full article: Traffic alert: Big change comes to Colonial Drive exit on I-4ORLANDO, Fla. – For the first time since the Florida Department of Transportation announced a traffic shift that would impact the 12,000 drivers who take the Colonial Drive exit on I-4 in the westbound lanes, rush hour traffic was put to the test Monday morning. The exit is now on the left side of the interstate and requires drivers to exit three-fourths of a mile sooner, after passing over New Hampshire Street, according to FDOT. "They’re everywhere out here.”The temporary exit will be in place for about a year as crews work on rebuilding the interchange at Colonial Drive and I-4. Officials purposely set the change for 10 p.m. Saturday to give drivers time to adapt to the change before the busy Monday morning commute. “Make sure you are paying attention and be ready for this shift,” Parks said.
Orlando officers injured in head-on crash, police say
Read full article: Orlando officers injured in head-on crash, police sayOrlando officers injured in head-on crash, police sayPublished: October 31, 2019, 6:56 amFour people, including two officers, were injured in a head-on crash involving an Orlando Police Department cruiser, according to the police department.
Flooding in Orlando leaves drivers stranded
Read full article: Flooding in Orlando leaves drivers strandedORLANDO, Fla. – Parts of Colonial Drive were covered with water Saturday night. Willis was driving home from work when she, like others, got stuck in the rising waters along Colonial Drive. One of the spots is N Pine Hills Rd/W Colonial Dr. — Vanessa Araiza (@AraizaReport) October 2, 2016Drivers were diverted to Pine Hills Road while Colonial Drive was closed. Traffic lights were out at Colonial Drive and Orange Blossom Trail. Colonial Drive reopened just after 9 p.m. Saturday.
Storm sewer work could affect traffic
Read full article: Storm sewer work could affect trafficORANGE/SEMINOLE, Fla. - Drivers in Orange and Seminole counties will see construction crews blocking at least one lane on several state roads as crews work to inspect and remove dirt and debris from storm sewer pipes. Details:• Nightly lane closures• From 7:00 p.m. to 7:00 a.m.• July 15 (Sunday night) to July 20 (Friday morning)State Roads:• Alafaya Trail (SR 434)From McCulloch Road (UCF Area) to Mitchell Hammock Road• Alafaya Trail (SR 434)From Colonial Drive (SR 50) to Centaurus West Drive (UCF Area)• Orange Avenue (SR 527) in the Orlando areaFrom Sand Lake Road (SR 482) to Gore Street• Alafaya Trail (SR 434)From US Highway 17/92 to SR 419• Kirkman Road (SR 435) in the Orlando areaFrom Colonial Drive (SR 50) to just south of the Florida Turnpike

article.text:
3 crashes Sunday leave 2 women, both 19, & a 64-year-old bicyclist dead
Read full article: 3 crashes Sunday leave 2 women, both 19, & a 64-year-old bicyclist deadThree crashes across Northeast Florida on Sunday left two 19-year-old women and a 64-year-old bicyclist dead, according to reports from the Florida Highway Patrol.
64-year-old bicyclist killed on US 1 in Flagler County: FHP
Read full article: 64-year-old bicyclist killed on US 1 in Flagler County: FHPA 64-year-old bicyclist from Ormond Beach died Sunday afternoon after he was struck by a pick-up truck on U.S. 1 at the Flagler-Volusia County line, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
Motorcyclist, 32, thrown from bike, dies after hitting debris on US 1 south in Flagler County: FHP
Read full article: Motorcyclist, 32, thrown from bike, dies after hitting debris on US 1 south in Flagler County: FHPA 32-year-old motorcyclist was thrown from his bike and died early Wednesday morning on U.S. 1 south of Belle Terre Boulevard in Flagler County, the Florida Highway Patrol said.
68-year-old from Savannah dies when pick-up truck veers into treeline off I-95 in St. Johns County: FHP
Read full article: 68-year-old from Savannah dies when pick-up truck veers into treeline off I-95 in St. Johns County: FHPA 68-year-old man died Monday evening when the pick-up truck he was driving hit several trees off I-95 south in St. Johns County, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
Woman driving wrong-way dies in head-on crash with pick-up truck on US 1: FHP
Read full article: Woman driving wrong-way dies in head-on crash with pick-up truck on US 1: FHPA 61-year-old woman who was driving the wrong way on U.S. 1 near I-95 Sunday night hit a pick-up truck head-on, the Florida Highway Patrol said.
66-year-old riding e-bike dies after head-on crash with SUV on US 1 in St. Johns County: FHP
Read full article: 66-year-old riding e-bike dies after head-on crash with SUV on US 1 in St. Johns County: FHPA 66-year-old St. Augustine man died Thursday night after his electronic bicycle crashed head on with an SUV on U.S. 1 in St. Johns County, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
Fatal motorcycle crash on US 1 at White View Parkway in Flagler County, FHP says
Read full article: Fatal motorcycle crash on US 1 at White View Parkway in Flagler County, FHP saysA 35-year-old motorcyclist was killed Friday evening in a crash on U.S. 1 in Flagler County, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
St. Johns County flag rescue program secures 20,000th American flag for dignified ceremony
Read full article: St. Johns County flag rescue program secures 20,000th American flag for dignified ceremonyMore than 20,000 American flags have been rescued from trash cans and collected from drop-off boxes throughout St. Johns County, thanks to a collaboration between veterans and the county’s Solid Waste Division.
Small plane makes emergency landing on US 1 in St. Johns County; no injuries reported
Read full article: Small plane makes emergency landing on US 1 in St. Johns County; no injuries reportedA small aircraft made an emergency landing Friday afternoon on U.S. 1 just north of the St. Augustine Airport, according to the St. Johns County Sheriff’s Office.
Traffic on King Street to be reduced to 1 lane overnight as crews work on water main improvement project
Read full article: Traffic on King Street to be reduced to 1 lane overnight as crews work on water main improvement projectThe City of St. Augustine announced on Thursday there will be overnight traffic pattern changes next week on King Street as crews continue work on the water main improvement project.
Wildfire in Nassau County may impact travel near Hilliard, FHP warns
Read full article: Wildfire in Nassau County may impact travel near Hilliard, FHP warnsThe Florida Highway Patrol is urging drivers to exercise caution due to an active wildfire burning near Kings Road and County Road 108 in Nassau County.
Deadly crash at U.S. 1 north and Pine Island Road: FHP
Read full article: Deadly crash at U.S. 1 north and Pine Island Road: FHPA person who had to be removed after being trapped in a car during a crash Wednesday morning in St. Johns County died at the hospital, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
1 killed in crash at intersection of US 1, Ray Road in St. Johns County: FHP
Read full article: 1 killed in crash at intersection of US 1, Ray Road in St. Johns County: FHPA 71-year-old man died Sunday night in a crash at the intersection of U.S. 1 and Ray Road in St. Johns County, the Florida Highway Patrol said.
Should St. Johns County build a 4-field croquet lawn at a St. Augustine park? They want to hear from residents
Read full article: Should St. Johns County build a 4-field croquet lawn at a St. Augustine park? They want to hear from residentsSt. Johns County has a plan to build a croquet lawn at a 2.3-acre park off U.S. 1 in St. Augustine.
Brush fire, smoky conditions shut down road leading in and out of Florida Keys
Read full article: Brush fire, smoky conditions shut down road leading in and out of Florida KeysTraffic is now flowing in and out of the Florida Keys after a brush fire led officials to briefly close U.S. 1, snarling traffic for hours for those trying to enter or leave the island chain.
89-year-old dies when truck hits mailbox, careens into ditch, then flips into multiple vehicles at Nassau County shop
Read full article: 89-year-old dies when truck hits mailbox, careens into ditch, then flips into multiple vehicles at Nassau County shopAn 89-year-old Jacksonville man was killed Monday morning when his pickup truck crashed in Nassau County, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
14-year-old driver thrown from car, critically injured in rollover crash on I-95 exit ramp
Read full article: 14-year-old driver thrown from car, critically injured in rollover crash on I-95 exit rampA 14-year-old boy was critically injured late Thursday night when he was thrown out of a car in a rollover crash on I-95 in Jacksonville, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
Man dies after being hit by car filled with 3 teens in St. Johns County: FHP
Read full article: Man dies after being hit by car filled with 3 teens in St. Johns County: FHPA 50-year-old St. Augustine man died Friday night after he stepped into the path of a car on U.S. 1 near Andora Street and was struck, the Florida Highway Patrol said.
Man killed when SUV flips into parking lot, hits him in Nassau County: FHP
Read full article: Man killed when SUV flips into parking lot, hits him in Nassau County: FHPA 32-year-old Hilliard man died Sunday when an SUV overturned in a crash on U.S. 1, flipped into a nearby parking lot and hit the man who was standing near a parked, empty Subaru, the Florida Highway Patrol said.
Georgia man dies in crash involving JTA bus on New Kings Road, FHP says
Read full article: Georgia man dies in crash involving JTA bus on New Kings Road, FHP saysA pickup truck driver died Thursday morning after crashing with a Jacksonville Transportation Authority passenger bus while traveling on U.S. 1, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
16-year-old charged after multi-county joyride with 5 other juveniles in stolen car, deputies say
Read full article: 16-year-old charged after multi-county joyride with 5 other juveniles in stolen car, deputies sayA 16-year-old is facing multiple felony charges after deputies said he drove a stolen car through St. Johns and Flagler counties with five other children along for the joyride.
1 killed when car crashes under side of semi on US 1 in St. Johns County
Read full article: 1 killed when car crashes under side of semi on US 1 in St. Johns CountyAn 84-year-old man from St. Johns was killed Tuesday morning in a crash on U.S. 1 at Pine Island Road, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
1 killed, 2 hurt in crash involving tractor-trailer, SUV on Philips Highway at Baymeadows Way: FHP
Read full article: 1 killed, 2 hurt in crash involving tractor-trailer, SUV on Philips Highway at Baymeadows Way: FHPA woman was killed and two people were hurt in a crash Friday morning involving a tractor-trailer and a sport utility vehicle on Philips Highway at Baymeadows Way, the Florida Highway Patrol said.
Semi, train involved in crash at US-1 & FL-16; no injuries reported
Read full article: Semi, train involved in crash at US-1 & FL-16; no injuries reportedSt. Johns County Fire Rescue on Friday night responded to a crash involving a semitruck and train near the intersection of State Road 16 and U.S. 1.
Woman, 51, killed in hit-and-run on US 1 in Flagler County: FHP
Read full article: Woman, 51, killed in hit-and-run on US 1 in Flagler County: FHPA 51-year-old woman was killed Saturday morning in a hit-and-run crash on U.S. 1 south of Plantation Bay Road in Flagler County, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
Man charged with DUI manslaughter nearly 2 years after fatal hit-and-run
Read full article: Man charged with DUI manslaughter nearly 2 years after fatal hit-and-runNearly two years after a deadly hit-and-run crash on U.S. 1 in St. Johns County, the Florida Highway Patrol announced Wednesday that a 65-year-old driver has been charged with DUI manslaughter.
I-TEAM: Nassau County property owner says he may have a solution to illegal street racing
Read full article: I-TEAM: Nassau County property owner says he may have a solution to illegal street racingThe owner of the Callahan Speedway contacted the News4JAX I-TEAM, saying he may soon have an option for drivers who have been speeding through the streets and parking lots of Jacksonville.
Pedestiran struck, killed on US1 in Callahan
Read full article: Pedestiran struck, killed on US1 in CallahanA pickup truck driven by a 19-year-old from Green Cove Springs struck a man walking on U.S. 1 at Cushman Road in Callahan on Wednesday night. According to the Florida Highway Patrol, the 41-year-old pedestrian died.
1 killed when truck, motorcycle crash on US 1 near I-295
Read full article: 1 killed when truck, motorcycle crash on US 1 near I-295At least one person died Tuesday morning in a crash involving a motorcycle at Philips Highway and Cedar Street, just north of the interchange with I-295, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
FHP: 3 bicyclists struck, 1 fatally, in St. Johns County
Read full article: FHP: 3 bicyclists struck, 1 fatally, in St. Johns CountyST. JOHNS COUNTY, Fla. – A man died and two women were seriously injured early Sunday morning when they were struck by a car while participating in a bicycle adventure race in St. Johns County, according to the Florida Highway Patrol. Troopers said the other two bicyclists -- a 46-year-old Illinois woman and a 41-year-old Missouri woman -- were seriously injured. Troopers said the driver of the car, a 27-year-old St. Augustine man, was not injured. Safety of all... Posted by Argeorgia on Sunday, February 28, 2021The accident happened right outside Michael Highsmith’s home. Highsmith said he ran outside with a flashlight and saw the three people spread far apart on the side of the road.
2 stopped in Jacksonville murder victim’s stolen car charged with theft, burglary
Read full article: 2 stopped in Jacksonville murder victim’s stolen car charged with theft, burglaryJACKSONVILLE, Fla. – Two suspects taken into custody in Nassau County on Tuesday night were in a stolen car belonging to one of two people found dead Monday in a home on Jacks Road in Northwest Jacksonville, according to News4Jax sources. According to the Nassau County Sheriff’s Office, deputies assisted Jacksonville Sheriff’s Office investigators with a stop at the Circle K on U.S. 1 near Ratliff Road at about 10:30 p.m.Nassau deputies said there were three young people -- two males and one female -- inside the vehicle when it was stopped. JSO jail records show only two of the trio were charged with vehicle theft and burglary. Sources told News4Jax that the two -- whose names and ages have not been released -- are not suspected of the killings. AdNeighbors in that Biltmore neighborhood said Purvis’ car was taken from their house the morning after they were killed and that one of the people later detained in the stolen car was one of Dean’s grandchildren.
Woman drives around cross arms, hit by oncoming train
Read full article: Woman drives around cross arms, hit by oncoming trainHILLIARD, Fla. – Railroad crossing arms and warning signals were going off on Andrews Road and U.S. 1 north in northern Nassau County when a 38-year-old woman continued to cross, according to the Florida Highway Patrol. Her car was hit by a southbound CSX train around 4:44 p.m. Friday. Her sedan was pushed off the tracks into an electrical box off to the side. The woman died. The CSX train activated its emergency stopping protocolTroopers are looking into the incident and working to identify the Hillard woman.
Day’s supply of COVID-19 vaccines in St. Johns County gone in 2½ hours
Read full article: Day’s supply of COVID-19 vaccines in St. Johns County gone in 2½ hoursAnother 383 vaccines were administered at the St. Johns County Fire-Rescue site. Quigley said more than 55,000 people 65 and above living in St. Johns County would qualify for the shots, but only 3,000 vaccines were distributed to the county so far. The line in St. John’s County is growing to try to get the first 3,000 of the county’s vaccines. @wjxt4 pic.twitter.com/kspLhKX0UC — Brittany Muller (@BrittMullerNews) January 1, 2021On Friday, St. Johns County Department of Health announced that anyone seeking a COVID-19 vaccination who takes prescribed blood thinner medications must bring a doctor’s note to receive a vaccination. To receive timely updates regarding the Department of Health in St. Johns County’s COVID-19 vaccination program, text SJCVACCINE to 888777.
Cars back up on US 1 as hundreds wait to get St. Johns County’s vaccinations
Read full article: Cars back up on US 1 as hundreds wait to get St. Johns County’s vaccinationsST. AUGUSTINE, Fla. – The Florida Department of Health in St. Johns County vaccinated people 65 years old and above for COVID-19 on Thursday, attracting a line of cars trying to reach its office off U.S. 1 north of State Road 16. “It would have been better if they had rolled this thing out with a plan, saying, ‘This is what you need to do,’” St. Johns County resident Michael Gurski said. Either the system wasn’t capable (or of) taking, I guess, the volume of calls.”To schedule a COVID-19 vaccination appointment in St. Johns County, visit stjohns.floridahealth.gov. For additional information regarding local vaccination operations, contact the Florida Department of Health in St. Johns County at 904-506-6081 or stjohnsfeedback@flhealth.gov. Visit the St. Johns County COVID-19 vaccinations webpage at www.sjcfl.us/coronavirusvaccinations or text SJCVACCINE to 888777 to receive updates.
Wrong way crash on I-95 in St. Johns County kills 1, injures 3
Read full article: Wrong way crash on I-95 in St. Johns County kills 1, injures 3It happened just after midnight on I-95 in between U.S. 1 and State Road 206, just north of the Flagler County line, according to a release from the Florida Highway Patrol. It was the latest of several head-on crashes caused by someone going the wrong way on the interstate. The man who died was the passenger in a sedan that state troopers believe was going the wrong way. Wallace “Wally” Beale was killed by a wrong-way driver on I-295 in Jacksonville nearly five years ago as he was driving home from work. News4Jax records show dozens of people have been killed in wrong way crashes in Northeast Florida.
What we know about St. Johns County’s COVID-19 vaccination plan
Read full article: What we know about St. Johns County’s COVID-19 vaccination planScores of cars in line to get COVID-19 vaccines at the St. Johns County Health Department early Thursday afternoon. Members of St. Johns County Fire Rescue also started receiving the first dose of the COVID-19 vaccine on Wednesday. St. Johns County and the Florida Department of Health in St. Johns County are working together on the first phase of vaccine distribution to the public. In the future, to schedule a COVID-19 vaccination appointment with the state Department of Health in St. Johns County, visit stjohns.floridahealth.gov. Visit the St. Johns County COVID-19 vaccinations webpage at www.sjcfl.us/coronavirusvaccinations or text SJCVACCINE to 888777 to receive updates.
Driver dies as Jeep hits tree, returns to U.S. 1 in Flagler County
Read full article: Driver dies as Jeep hits tree, returns to U.S. 1 in Flagler CountyFLAGLER COUNTY, Fla. – A Jeep SUV headed north on U.S. 1 in Flagler County, north of Royal Palms Parkway early Saturday morning ran off the road into the median and collided with a tree. The Jeep ended up back on the northbound lanes of U.S. 1, where it came to a final stop in an intersection. The 61-year-old driver from Deland died in the single-vehicle crash at 12:32 a.m. Troopers said he was not wearing a seatbelt. The crash remains under investigation.
Palm Coast woman crossing US-1 struck, killed by car
Read full article: Palm Coast woman crossing US-1 struck, killed by carPALM COAST, Fla. – A woman driving south on U.S. 1 in Flagler County struck and killed a pedestrian just after 9 p.m. Saturday. According to the Florida Highway Patrol, the 55-year-old pedestrian died at the scene. Troopers think the woman, a Palm Coast resident, was trying to cross the highway just south of Royal Palms Parkway. The 33-year-old Palatka driver stopped and was cooperating with authorities. FHP is investigating the incident.
FHP: Car that drove off located in fatal U.S. 1 hit-and-run
Read full article: FHP: Car that drove off located in fatal U.S. 1 hit-and-runST. JOHNS COUNTY, Fla. One northbound lane of U.S. 1 in St. Augustine Shores was blocked Wednesday afternoon as the Florida Highway Patrol investigated a hit-and-run crash that killed one person and injured a second. They said a man rear-ended a car in front of him, causing it to overturn. The driver of the other car continued driving about a mile, pulling behind a BB&T Bank to conceal the car. The man was located by St. Johns County deputies, and he was hospitalized. Troopers have not released any information about the people who were inside the car that overturned.
Teen airlifted to hospital after crash in Nassau County
Read full article: Teen airlifted to hospital after crash in Nassau CountyHILLIARD, Fla. – A teenage boy was airlifted to a Jacksonville hospital following a two-vehicle crash Saturday night near Hilliard, the Florida Highway Patrol said. Just before 9 p.m., according to FHP, a 35-year-old Hilliard man and a 13-year-old Hilliard boy were in a pickup truck traveling west on Murrhee Road, approaching U.S. 1. The tractor-trailer spun through the road’s median and across the southbound lanes before finally coming to a rest on the west shoulder of U.S. 1. The Highway Patrol said the teenage passenger in the pickup was airlifted to UF Health Jacksonville with critical injuries. Troopers said the driver of the pickup truck suffered minor injuries and the 29-year-old Jacksonville man driving the tractor-trailer was not hurt.
SJC DUI arrest
Read full article: SJC DUI arrestST. AUGUSTINE, Fla. A 56-year-old man was booked into the St. Johns County Jail on Wednesday, accused of neglecting manslaughter with a vehicle and DUI. According to reports, Kevin Adams is charged in connection with a crash Sunday night on U.S. 1 that resulted in the death of a motorcyclist. Historic City News reported that Adams was making a U-turn on U.S. 1 at Moses Creek Boulevard when he pulled in the path of motorcyclist Brandon Carter, 20, of St. Augustine. The motorcycle caught fire and Carter died at the scene. Adams is being held on $51,500 bond.
Troopers: 11-year-old killed in crash on US 1
Read full article: Troopers: 11-year-old killed in crash on US 1ST. JOHNS COUNTY, Fla. An 11-year-old St. Augustine girl was killed in a single-vehicle crash Sunday afternoon on U.S. 1 in St. Johns County, the Florida Highway Patrol said. According to the Highway Patrol, an SUV was traveling north in the left lane of U.S. 1 about 3:10 p.m. when the driver attempted to go around a large puddle of water and lost control of the vehicle. Troopers said the SUV then crossed the median and overturned, ejecting the 11-year-old passenger. Troopers said the girl died. The driver, a 32-year-old St. Augustine woman, suffered minor injuries, the Highway Patrol said.
Deputies: 2 injured after crash involving bicycle on US 1
Read full article: Deputies: 2 injured after crash involving bicycle on US 1ST. JOHNS COUNTY, Fla. Two people were injured, one critically, in a crash Wednesday involving a bicycle on U.S. 1, according to the St. Johns County Sheriffs Office. The Florida Highway Patrol website shows it happened about 11:10 a.m. near Regalo Road. Its unclear exactly what happened, but a photo of the scene shows a bicycle on the ground and a vehicle that overturned. The Sheriffs Office said the critical patient will be flown to a hospital and the other one will be taken by ambulance.
Mother mourns 'miracle baby' son after couple killed on US 301
Read full article: Mother mourns 'miracle baby' son after couple killed on US 301Troopers said the crash, which killed Micah Phoenix, 37, and Yomadith Rodriguez Matos, 32, occurred shortly after 11 p.m. on 301 near Southwest 89th Street in Bradford County. Phoenix's mother, Christina Johnson, told News4Jax that the family was watching TV when the couple decided to go for a walk. Johnson said an officer knocked on her door at 3 a.m. with the news that her son and his girlfriend had been killed. Rodriguez Matos and Phoenix died at the scene of the crash, which was just 2 miles from his home. Family said the two had been dating for about a month, and Rodriguez Matos' brother told News4Jax she leaves behind three daughters, ages 12, 10 and 8.
Fugitive captured in Florida after 4 years on the run
Read full article: Fugitive captured in Florida after 4 years on the runBUNNELL, Fla. - Deputies say the search for a fugitive who was on the run for four years came to an end Wednesday when he was captured after fleeing from a Flagler County traffic stop. Out of concern for public safety, the deputy slowed down to follow the vehicle from a safe distance, according to an arrest report. Deputies said the driver scrambled out of the car and took off running. The foot chase was short-lived, however, as a deputy stopped him in his tracks using an agency-issued Taser. The driver was identified as 24-year-old Michael Anthony Ross, who was wanted on a Wisconsin warrant for violating probation in an armed robbery case, deputies said.
K-9 rewarded with toy after finding drugs tossed from stolen car
Read full article: K-9 rewarded with toy after finding drugs tossed from stolen carST. AUGUSTINE BEACH, Fla. - A St. Augustine Beach Police Department K-9 was rewarded after he helped the St. Johns County Sheriff's Office search for drugs tossed from a stolen vehicle, authorities said. After a woman threw Xanax tablets and methamphetamine from a stolen vehicle on U.S. 1 on Tuesday, according to a police report, K-9 Kilo began to search the area for drugs. The police report shows Kilo found an orange pill bottle with tablets and a clear plastic baggie in the grass. Kilo was then rewarded with a Kong toy. In March, News4Jax reported that Kilo was saved thanks to a donated heat sensor.
Deputies: Florida man flashes gun at woman uninterested in vape pen
Read full article: Deputies: Florida man flashes gun at woman uninterested in vape penST. AUGUSTINE, Fla. - A St. Johns County man is accused of pulling a gun on a woman who wouldnt try his vape pen. The confrontation happened Friday morning at the McDonalds on U.S. 1 in St. Augustine where the woman works, according to an arrest report. He said a stranger had flashed a handgun after he refused to try out the mans vape pen. Deputies said the woman identified him on sight and recognized the weapon, a .45-caliber handgun, as the one shown to her before. Deputies identified the suspect as Kyle McGill Walker, 19, of St. Augustine.
Woman killed in crash with semi in Nassau County
Read full article: Woman killed in crash with semi in Nassau CountyHILLIARD, Fla. - A 29-year-old woman was killed Thursday morning when her car collided with a tractor-trailer on U.S. 1 in Nassau County, the Florida Highway Patrol said. According to the FHP report, the woman, whose name has not been released yet, pulled out of a private driveway onto U.S. 1 into the path of the semi and was hit. The driver of the semi was not injured, troopers said. The woman died at the scene. She was wearing a seat belt.
Woman accused of driving drunk, high in crash that killed father of 2
Read full article: Woman accused of driving drunk, high in crash that killed father of 2ST. JOHNS COUNTY, Fla. - A 54-year-old woman was driving impaired and speeding without her headlights on when she crashed her SUV into a pickup truck in February, killing a father of two, the Florida Highway Patrol said. Grace Ann King has been charged with DUI manslaughter, vehicular homicide, DUI property damage and driving without headlights. The crash was reported Feb. 4 on U.S. 1 in St. Johns County. According to the FHP report, toxicology results showed King was "under the influence of both alcohol and controlled substances at the time of the crash." News4Jax found several previous citations for King in St. Johns County, including speeding six times and twice driving with a suspended or revoked license.
Ponte Vedra woman wins part of $650K jackpot
Read full article: Ponte Vedra woman wins part of $650K jackpotJACKSONVILLE, Fla. – A Ponte Vedra woman won half of the $650,000 jackpot from last month's Lucky Money lottery drawing, lottery officials announced Tuesday. Carolyn Morris, 70, bought her winning ticket at the GATE store on U.S. 1 near County Road 210. The other winning ticket was purchased in Zephyrhills. Morris took her prize money as a lump sum payment of $245,390, lottery officials said. The next Lucky Money drawing is scheduled for Tuesday at 11:15 p.m.
Boil water notice canceled in Hilliard
Read full article: Boil water notice canceled in HilliardHILLIARD, Fla. – A boil water notice for residents of Hilliard in Nassau County was canceled Thursday. The precautionary advisory was issued Tuesday after a water main broke at U.S. 1 and First Avenue, Town of Hilliard Public Works Director David Thompson said. Residents were advised to use bottled water or bring water to a rolling boil of one minute before using for drinking, cooking, making ice, brushing teeth or washing dishes. The notice remained in effect until the water main was repaired and a bacteriological survey showed that the water is safe to drink.The order was rescinded at 1:30 p.m. Thursday. Anyone with questions can call the town hall at 904-845-3555 or 904-813-3314.

article.title: 1 killed in crash on US 1 in Brevard County, troopers say
article.text:
BREVARD COUNTY, Fla. – A fatal crash on Saturday forced the temporary closure of southbound lanes on U.S. 1 in Brevard County, according to the Florida Highway Patrol.
A 2013 Subaru Outback was exiting a parking lot across U.S. 1 from Otter Creek Lane as a 2020 Nissan Altima was traveling northbound on U.S. 1, approaching Otter Creek Lane, troopers said.
Recommended Videos
As the Outback’s driver attempted to cross the northbound lanes of U.S. 1, the Subaru entered the direct path of the Nissan and its left side was struck by the front of the Altima, FHP said in a statement.
The Subaru’s driver was hospitalized and pronounced dead, troopers said, as the Nissan’s driver suffered minor injuries.
There was a roadblock in place for the southbound lanes of U.S. 1, but the road is back open, according to the FHP.

article.title: St. Johns County deputy thrown from motorcycle in crash on 210
article.text:
A St. Johns County deputy was seriously hurt after he crashed his motorcycle on County Road 210 just west of I-95.
Deputy Tony Deleo, 49, has been with the St. Johns County Sheriff's Office for three years. Deleo has serious -- but not life-threatening -- injuries.
The accident happened around 2:15 p.m. on a curve on 210 parallel to Cimarrone Golf Club.
Troopers said Deleo was driving westbound when he traveled off the right side of the road and hit a tree.
Deleo was thrown off the motorcycle and hit a concrete power line pole.
Troopers are investigating.
Just arriving on scene. Looks like the motorcycle slammed into the pole. Debris is all around the crash area @ActionNewsJax pic.twitter.com/RnpvBr1WCH
— Danielle Avitable (@Danielle_onTV) July 14, 2017

article.title: 71-year-old pedestrian hit by pickup on University Drive, Coral Springs cops say
article.text:
A 71-year-old man was crossing a street when he was struck by a pickup truck in Coral Springs, police said.
The crash happened at around 2:08 p.m. on Tuesday, March 17, on University Drive and NW 20th Street, just south of the intersection at Royal Palm Boulevard, according to police.
A pedestrian was walking across the street in a crosswalk when a vehicle hit him. It’s unclear who had the right of way at the time. No one was in custody as of the afternoon of March 17.
The victim was rushed to Broward Health North with critical injuries, but he’s in stable condition, according to police.
The accident closed the northbound lanes of University Drive at the intersection for about two hours, then the road was re-opened by 4:15 p.m.
The investigation is ongoing.
article.author: Olivia Lloyd

article.title: 3 students got minor scrapes in crash at Fort Pierce Boulevard, Winter Garden Parkway
article.text:
LAKEWOOD PARK — Two drivers were taken to a hospital Wednesday afternoon following a crash involving a St. Lucie Public Schools bus and a truck, according to officials.
Three students on the school bus sustained minor scrapes and were turned over to their parents after the crash at Fort Pierce Boulevard and Winter Garden Parkway, according to Lt. Andrew Bolonka, St. Lucie County Sheriff’s Office spokesperson. Deputies went to the scene in the northern part of the county about 2:20 p.m.
Lydia Martin, schools spokesperson, confirmed the vehicle was a St. Lucie Public Schools bus.
Bolonka said the bus traveled north on Fort Pierce Boulevard as the truck headed northwest across Fort Pierce Boulevard when the crash occurred.
The truck failed to yield to oncoming traffic, Bolonka said.
The bus driver and truck driver were taken to a hospital, but their conditions were not immediately known late Wednesday.
Only three children were on the bus, Bolonka said.
No other information was immediately available.

article.title: Person of interest identified in fatal Alachua County hit-and-run
article.text:
A person of interest was identified following a fatal hit-and-run that killed a Floral City man walking along US Highway 441 early Sunday morning in Alachua County.
According to a Florida Highway Patrol (FHP) release, a 2015-20 dark grey Ford Mustang driving south hit and killed the 26-year-old Floral City man walking along US 441 just south of SW 104th Avenue around 3:30 a.m. The vehicle continued south toward Micanopy and Marion County.
Around 3 p.m., FHP investigators located the vehicle and identified a person of interest.
Anyone with information about this crash is asked to call 850-960-9743, FHP Dispatch at 1-800-387-1290 or the local Crime Stoppers.

article.title: State Road 82: A road marked by tragedy and a plea for safety improvements
article.text:
State Road 82, specifically the stretch between Interstate 75 and Immokalee Road, is not just a highway; it’s a place marked by tragedy and danger.
For many, like Chris Rahmings, it’s a road that represents profound loss and persistent fear.
Rahmings lost her husband, Leroy, in a fiery crash on SR-82 in 2010.
Leroy, a 15-year veteran of the Collier County Sheriff’s Office, was on his way to work when a semi-truck crashed into his pickup truck on SR-82 near Blackstone Drive.
Leroy was burned beyond recognition, leaving Chris devastated.
Chris never received his wedding ring or any other personal items, as everything was destroyed in the fiery crash.
Every time Chris drives on SR-82, she feels intense anxiety and has panic attacks. She believes installing traffic signals at intersections along the road could prevent crashes like the one that took her husband’s life.
Though that crash happened over a decade ago, Chris and her daughter Alana Cordero, believe too many people have died since.
“It’s just a slap in the face to the families that this continues to happen,” said Cordero.
Root of the issue
According to LCSO traffic unit Sgt. Nicholas Duncan, he and his team respond to one or two deadly crashes a month on SR-82.
Lee County Sheriff’s Office’s traffic unit considers SR-82 one of the most dangerous roads due to the high volume of traffic and speeding.
“It’s one of the main roads that can cut through almost the entire state,” said Duncan, “so you can go from one end of the state to the other. It is heavily traveled and because of that we just needed to make it as safe as possible.”
Where it stands now, drivers are able to turn left at certain intersections on SR-82.
To do that, drivers have to cross multiple lanes of traffic.
“When you have three lanes to travel across, plus a large center median or something like that, there’s a lot that you need to pay attention to,” said Duncan. “Not being able to make a lefthand turn, I think, would be a lifesaver, honestly, because there have been many times where I’ve worked fatal crashes or serious crashes. It’s because people make lefthand turns.”
Rahmings believes traffic signals are the only answer, other than people slowing down.
“Florida Department of Transportation, do something about it. You hold the key. It’s taken too long,” said Rahmings.
FDOT’s proposal
In April, FDOT held a meeting on SR-82 proposing a safety project.
The project proposes interim median modifications to reduce conflict points and increase safety at the intersections of SR-82 with Sunshine Boulevard, Alabama Road, Blackstone Drive/Grant Boulevard, Rue Labeau Circle, Parkdale Boulevard, Bell Boulevard and Columbus Boulevard.
The proposed safety improvements will prohibit left turns from these side streets onto SR-82.
Traffic will be required to turn right onto SR-82 and make a U-turn at one of the adjacent intersections.
In an email to WINK News, a spokesperson for FDOT District One wrote:
FDOT District One has been studying crash data in the SR82 corridor and have identified 7 intersections to potentially install temporary curbing to directionalize these intersections. We held a public meeting April 11th to take comment and seek local knowledge and we are continuing to seek finding for both temporary and permanent solutions. One of the seven intersections (Sunshine Blvd) will be improved to a traffic signal similar to the (82 and Daniels) intersection, which is a displaced left. The county will be improving that intersection with the extension of Alico Rd. That would not necessarily disqualify Sunshine Blvd. from the temporary curbing as an interim fix.
In summary, FDOT plans to improve safety on SR-82 by making intersection median modifications. They will prohibit left turns onto SR-82 from side streets, directing traffic to turn right and make U-turns at adjacent intersections instead.
FDOT is also considering installing temporary curbing at these intersections and upgrading the Sunshine Boulevard intersection to a traffic signal.
What’s the timeline?
FDOT plans to install the interim median modification during the summer of 2024. Lee County’s current plans are to begin construction of the Alico Road extension in midâ2026, which will include the reconstruction of the Sunshine Boulevard intersection. FDOT will continue to evaluate ultimate intersection alternatives, to include traffic signals and other intersection configurations that will reduce leftâturn conflicts, at the remaining six intersections.
Cracking down on enforcement
WINK News Anchor Emma Heaton sat in the passenger seat of Duncan’s patrol car during a team traffic stop operation.
During the traffic unit’s patrol, deputies pulled over multiple vehicles, including one driver under the age of 21 caught speeding at 80 mph in a 55 mph zone.
“How often are you guys doing operations like this?” asked WINK News anchor Emma Heaton.
“During the day, we pretty much do an operation a day. Sometimes, if the schedules allow we try to get multiple operations each day,” responded Sergeant Duncan.
Duncan said a lot of the operations are centered around citizen complaints. Other times, the team looks into hotspots for the week to make efforts known, the traffic unit is out and enforcing laws to keep everyone safe.
Duncan believes deadly crashes like that, could be prevented with FDOT’s plan to take away left turns and that the addition of traffic signals would help slow drivers down.
Sit down with Sheriff Carmine Marceno
Lee County Sheriff Carmine Marceno announced Thursday the newly rebranded traffic unit’s effort to improve safety on the road and all roads in Lee County.
The rebranding for LCSO Highway Patrol is expected to make SR 82 safer by increasing enforcement, education and awareness efforts.
“We’re going to be everywhere, everywhere we can be and people are going to learn,” said Marceno. “A lot of times you give people a verbal warning or a written warning right, and you want to educate them and gain compliance.”
The sheriff said some drivers ignore warnings and become repeat offenders.
No more.
“Those people will be cited,” said Marceno. “We are going to give tickets out to people that don’t understand. You got your warning. We’re trying to educate you. Now it’s enforcement time, and this enforcement is going to be everywhere. People have to slow down.”
The sheriff believes the revamped unit will be more proactive and visible, deploying resources to address traffic concerns and enforce laws.
The increased presence and enforcement aims to deter speeding, distracted driving and other behaviors that contribute to crashes.
Additionally, the unit’s focus on education and engagement is intended to promote safer driving habits among residents and visitors.
article.author: Emma Heaton

Case Number : 2003-15958
- Date/Time: June 4, 2003 2:16 a.m.
- Location: 7500 Block of 49 Street, Pinellas Park, FL
- Victim: Mark Alan Reed, White Male, Age: 29
On June 4, 2003 at 2:16 a.m., the victim, Mark Reed, was struck in front of 7490 49 Street by a hit and run vehicle, as he crossed the roadway from the east side to the west side. Pinellas Park Police request help from anyone who might have witnessed the accident or have information regarding the driver of the hit and run vehicle. The suspect's vehicle was described as a maroon pick up truck, possibly an older model Chevrolet S10. The driver of the vehicle was described as a white male. The truck should have front end damage consistent with striking a pedestrian.
Please direct all information and leads to Detective M. Piacenza at 727-369-7864 or Email Officer Piacenza at mpiacenza@pinellas-park.com
Case Number : 2005-8391
- Date/Time: March 17, 2005 2 a.m.
- Location: 7500 Block of 49 Street, Pinellas Park, FL
- Victim: Raymond J Castles, White, Male, Age: 50
- Vehicle Description: Newer White, 2 Door Car.
- Suspect Description: Unknown
On March 17, 2005 at about 11:45 a.m., the body of the victim was located in the parking lot of 5001 Park Boulevard It was discovered the victim was struck at the 7500 block of 49 Street, while walking in or along the roadway, and possibly crawled to the location where he was found. The suspect vehicle was last scene heading northbound on 49 Street. It is unknown where the damage to the suspect’s vehicle would be. If the vehicle is seen or you have information about the vehicle or driver, please contact us.
Case Number : 2005-23283
- Date/Time: August 5, 2005 6:30 a.m.
- Location: 66th Street (SR 693), 226 feet south of Park Boulevard (SR 694), Pinellas Park, FL
- Victim: Arthur E Maurer, white, male, 61
This case involves the hit and run of a pedestrian named Arthur Maurer. At approximately 6:30 a.m. on August 5th, 2005, Arthur Maurer attempted to cross 66th Street just south of the Park Boulevard intersection. Heather Forcier was driving her Red Chevy Cavalier southbound on 66th Street in the median lane. As she passed Arthur Maurer, her passenger side mirror struck him spinning him around. A white mini-van traveling behind Forcier’s Cavalier then struck Maurer head-on causing massive injuries to Maurer’s body. As Arthur Maurer laid in the roadway, both vehicles stopped at the scene. Heather Forcier exited her vehicle and remained at the scene. The driver of the white mini-van and a passenger both exited their vehicle to look at Maurer, but quickly re-entered their vehicle and left the scene southbound without providing anyone with their identity. Arthur Maurer was taken to Bayfront Medical Hospital where he died of blunt trauma to his head and torso sustained when the mini-van struck him
The driver of the mini-van was described by witnesses as a heavy set white female, late 30’s to early 40’s in age, shoulder length blond hair. She was wearing white shorts and a yellow shirt, with sandals. The passenger was described as a late teens to early 20’s, white male with blond hair, wearing a white shirt and black pants.
The mini-van was described as possibly being a white Toyota Sienna, with tan or gold trim around the bottom of the door panels. The tag had the digits IT9 or I9T possibly as the last three digits. The van would have had some front end damage or denting.
Case: 2010-26876
- Hit and Run Serious Bodily Injury (Amputation)
- Date/Time: October 10, 2010 10 p.m.
- Location: 49 Street and 86 Avenue, Pinellas Park, FL
- Victim: Karri Bush White, Female, 20
Vehicle Description: 2001 - 2003 Toyota Highlander, Standard or Limited Edition, Silver in Color, Heavy Front Damage, Missing or Replaced Front Grill, Possibly Roof/Luggage Rack.
Suspect Description: Unknown
On the above listed date and time, an unknown suspect struck the victim, Karri Bush, with a motor vehicle while she was riding her bicycle. The victim was launched to the roof and held onto the roof for over a mile before the suspect made a u-turn, throwing the victim off the roof onto the pavement. The victim suffered severe lacerations / fractures to her left leg which had to be amputated. Year, Make, and Model were identified from the vehicle’s front Grill which was left behind. An anonymous tip reports the vehicle was possibly repaired / being repaired in the Clearwater / Lakeview Road / Greenwood area. If the vehicle is seen or you have information about the vehicle or the driver, please contact us.
Case: 2011-43108
- Hit and Run Involving Death
- Date/Time: October 8, 2011 8:37 p.m.
- Location: 78th Avenue / 66th Street
- Victim: Pamela Collier
- Vehicle Description: Silver in color
- Suspect Description: Unknown
On October 8, 2011 at approximately 2037 hours an unknown vehicle was traveling east on 78 Avenue approaching 66 Street. At that time Pamela Collier was riding her mobility assistance scooter south across 78 Avenue at 66 Street in the crosswalk. The unknown vehicle struck Collier and her scooter, launching her into the center of the intersection. The vehicle fled the scene in an unknown direction of travel. Witnesses were unable to give a vehicle description. Silver Metallic paint strips were found embedded in the right side of the scooter Collier was operating. The unknown vehicle is Silver Metallic in color at the front. It is not known if the entire vehicle is this color or not. Mrs. Collier died as a result of her injuries that night.
Tips & Information
Sergeant Z. Huff
7700 59 Street North
Pinellas Park, FL 33781
Phone: 727-369-7865
Email Tips and Information traffictips@pinellas-park.com

article.title: FHP officials respond to a tractor trailer and vehicle crash in Bay County
article.text:
BAY COUNTY, FL. (WMBB) – The Florida Highway Patrol responded to a crash involving a tractor-trailer and vehicle on Friday.
According to a news release, the incident happened at Latitude Margaritaville near the intersection of State Road 79 and SR 388, just after 7 a.m.
Troopers said a 62-year-old woman from Daleville Alabama driving an SUV made a left on SR 388 from SR 79 and a 46-year-old man from Pace Florida driving a tractor-trailer was traveling south in the outside lane of State Road 79.
Troopers said the SUV failed to yield to the right of way, colliding with the tractor-trailer.
As a result, the SUV overturned, and the tractor-trailer continued traveling in a southern direction and overturned.
The tractor-trailer came to a final rest on its left side in the southbound lanes of SR79 facing east, officials wrote.
Troopers confirmed both drivers sustained only minor injuries.
article.author: Melaniemitchell

Fatal crash: Dodge Charger slams into wall near I-4 in Seminole County
Read full article: Fatal crash: Dodge Charger slams into wall near I-4 in Seminole CountySEMINOLE COUNTY, Fla. – A fatal crash is under investigation Thursday after the driver of a Dodge Charger crashed into a wall near I-4 in Seminole County, the Florida Highway Patrol said. The wreck was reported on the exit ramp from I-4 east to the State Road 46 exit ramp. According to the FHP crash report, the 51-year-old driver of a Dodge Charger drove onto the exit ramp, left the roadway and ran into the wall supporting the overpass. The driver was pronounced dead at the scene of the crash. The crash remains under investigation.
Contained gas leak closes westbound SR 46 in Seminole County
Read full article: Contained gas leak closes westbound SR 46 in Seminole CountySEMINOLE COUNTY, Fla. Seminole County Fire Department hazmat teams responded to a gas leak Tuesday morning on State Road 46. The leak was contained by the hazmat crew but westbound SR 46 was closed around 10 a.m. from Elder Road to Rinehart Road. No other information was provided. Check back for updates.

- Date/Time: October 10, 2010 10 p.m.
- Location: 49 Street and 86 Avenue, Pinellas Park, FL
- Victim: Karri Bush White, Female, 20
Vehicle Description: 2001 - 2003 Toyota Highlander, Standard or Limited Edition, Silver in Color, Heavy Front Damage, Missing or Replaced Front Grill, Possibly Roof/Luggage Rack.
Suspect Description: Unknown
On the above listed date and time, an unknown suspect struck the victim, Karri Bush, with a motor vehicle while she was riding her bicycle. The victim was launched to the roof and held onto the roof for over a mile before the suspect made a u-turn, throwing the victim off the roof onto the pavement. The victim suffered severe lacerations / fractures to her left leg which had to be amputated. Year, Make, and Model were identified from the vehicle’s front Grill which was left behind. An anonymous tip reports the vehicle was possibly repaired / being repaired in the Clearwater / Lakeview Road / Greenwood area. If the vehicle is seen or you have information about the vehicle or the driver, please contact us.
Case: 2011-43108
- Hit and Run Involving Death
- Date/Time: October 8, 2011 8:37 p.m.
- Location: 78th Avenue / 66th Street
- Victim: Pamela Collier
- Vehicle Description: Silver in color
- Suspect Description: Unknown
On October 8, 2011 at approximately 2037 hours an unknown vehicle was traveling east on 78 Avenue approaching 66 Street. At that time Pamela Collier was riding her mobility assistance scooter south across 78 Avenue at 66 Street in the crosswalk. The unknown vehicle struck Collier and her scooter, launching her into the center of the intersection. The vehicle fled the scene in an unknown direction of travel. Witnesses were unable to give a vehicle description. Silver Metallic paint strips were found embedded in the right side of the scooter Collier was operating. The unknown vehicle is Silver Metallic in color at the front. It is not known if the entire vehicle is this color or not. Mrs. Collier died as a result of her injuries that night.
Tips & Information
Sergeant Z. Huff
7700 59 Street North
Pinellas Park, FL 33781
Phone: 727-369-7865
Email Tips and Information traffictips@pinellas-park.com
